most_helpful: one of the most helpful and educational aspects of my practicum agency was the opportunity to apply classroom knowledge in real world settings while being supported by experienced supervisors and colleagues. i was able to practice a wide range of generalist and integrated skills, including case management, client advocacy, crisis intervention, and interdisciplinary collaboration. the agency’s commitment to serving diverse populations, particularly refugees and immigrants, deepened my understanding of cultural humility, trauma informed care, and systemic barriers to services. regular supervision sessions were especially valuable, providing me with constructive feedback, ethical guidance, and opportunities for self reflection, which helped me grow both professionally and personally.
least_helpful: the least helpful aspect was the occasional lack of structure around certain projects and tasks. at times, there was limited clarity on expectations or deadlines, which made it challenging to prioritize or fully understand the purpose of some assignments. while this did push me to advocate for more direction and practice professional communication, having more structured onboarding or clearer project guidelines would have enhanced the overall learning experience.
recommend_reason: the agency offers rich learning opportunities for students interested in working with diverse and vulnerable populations, particularly refugees and immigrants. it provides practical experience in generalist practice, interdisciplinary collaboration, and advocacy work. however, i would advise future students to be proactive in seeking clarification, supervision, and structure when needed to make the most of the experience.
comments_supervisor: my afi played a crucial role in my professional development by fostering a supportive and growth oriented environment. their mentorship encouraged me to think critically, reflect on my practice, and build confidence in my abilities as a future social worker. i appreciated the balance of guidance and autonomy provided throughout my practicum experience.
other_comments: overall, my practicum experience was invaluable in developing my social work practice. i was able to build a strong foundation in generalist practice, particularly in working with diverse and marginalized populations. the support from my afi and the field education team helped me navigate challenges and grow as a professional. this experience has deepened my commitment to social justice and advocacy, and i feel well-prepared for my future career as a social worker.

recommend_reason: i would recommend this practicum placement to another student because it is a great learning environment with people who support your growth as a social worker. i learned valuable skills in prevention and working with others. i loved working at the micro level of social work and understanding how the university system works as a whole.
other_comments: i had a great first practicum experience. the peace project was a valuable agency on campus that taught me so many educational lessons about social work and gave me a valuable perspective on how i want to be as a social worker going forward.

other_comments: the last month or so of my practicum, my role was completely shifted. i was asked to take lead in a role that i believe should have taken much more time. the role was a temporary position for me, as another person was being hired to take over, so i felt slightly taken advantage of. i did agree to take on the position, so perhaps i could have put up a better boundary. when i experienced a major loss the last couple weeks of the semester, i had to really double down at my practicum to 'finish' the job, while also trying to take care of myself, and felt very overwhelmed with navigating my grief, school, and an intense new role, while attending to my previous clients. .

most_helpful: they provided intensive training about their programs, which set students up for success and gave them many opportunities to gain hands-on experiences. this was the most helpful!
least_helpful: many clients are working, so meeting with clients will require students to be flexible. students may need to meet clients outside of regular work hours, which can pose a challenge for some.
recommend_reason: the practicum site provides students with many opportunities for professional growth, and students will gain a great deal from it.

most_helpful: understanding the importance of the multidisciplinary collaboration to support clients in acute crisis has been rewarding. not only do i know the roles of social workers, rns, mhts, and psychiatrists, i learned how to advocate for clients in a fast paced, environment. i also have learned how to navigate ethical tensions, and learning to balance work-life balance. it was eye-opening to see the systems level and the need to bridge inpatient care with community support! i started this practicum living in a new city, and learning what the community has to offer for its patients was crucial in my development.
least_helpful: due to the fast-paced nature of inpatient psychiatric care, much of the work is short term with stabilization being the top priority. i understand and agree that stabilization is important, sometimes i felt like there wasn't enough time for me to truly build rapport, or witness long term client process. i love the past paced nature, so this is the only thing that comes to mind!
recommend_reason: yes! this practicum has deepened my understanding of systemic barriers individuals with mental health crisis face- whether that is those who are unhoused, struggle with substance use, or compounding risk factors, involvement with criminal justice system. this job opportunity provided me with clinical experience i might've not otherwise witnessed. it also has fostered a deep sense of empathy and commitment to my community. i also learned how interprofessional collaboration works and how important teamwork is for patient success.
comments_supervisor: mandie has gone above and beyond for the development of not only my success but my coworkers! she has made my experience with lhbh unforgettable. she always had an open door and answered any questions that came up. supervision has helped me navigate difficult situations on the unit including becoming more confident in my voice, hipaa policies, trauma informed care, navigating clients in crisis, and the important of taking care of ourselves outside of work.

most_helpful: i found a few things helpful about my practicum. first, i interacted with a lot of clients who were in the lowest moments, not knowing what they would do next. it was beneficial to continuously engage in the problem-solving process. additionally, i felt it was helpful to be involved in the day-to-day functioning of a nonprofit organization, as we are not limited to one role.
least_helpful: i found each day at the organization helpful, and i don't really have a solid answer about what was least helpful.

most_helpful: one of the most educational aspects of my practicum at on target aba has been gaining a deeper understanding of how to implement trauma-informed care and motivational interviewing (mi) within aba therapy. observing how staff engage with children on the autism spectrum, as well as their families and school teams, has highlighted the importance of collaboration and individualized support. i have also learned that incorporating clients in their own behavior plans leads to better outcomes, though this may be case by case. additionally, working directly with families has reinforced the value of active listening and patience, especially when navigating challenging behaviors or discussing progress.
least_helpful: one of the more challenging parts of my practicum at on target aba has been navigating the balance between aba’s structured approach and my own values around client-centered care. i’ve seen how involving clients in their own behavior plans leads to better outcomes, but not everyone seems open to that idea. there are also moments when the focus on compliance feels a little at odds with approaches like empowerment and self-determination, which i really value. it’s been frustrating at times, but it’s also made me think more critically about how to bridge the gap between aba and a more holistic, strengths-based perspective.
recommend_reason: yes, i’d recommend my practicum agency to another student with similar goals, but with a few things to keep in mind. on target aba offers great hands-on experience working with children on the autism spectrum, plus a chance to build clinical skills and see how behavior plans play out in real life. it’s also a great place to learn how to work with families and school teams. that said, the structured nature of aba can sometimes feel a bit rigid, especially if you’re more drawn to client-centered or strengths-based approaches.

most_helpful: working with immigrant families at the family resource center taught me about the challenges they face, like accessing resources and building connections. it improved my cultural awareness, communication skills, and ability to help families find the support they need.
least_helpful: there wasn’t anything at the family resource center that i found unhelpful or not educational. every experience contributed to my learning, whether through direct practice, observation, or reflection.
recommend_reason: the family resource center is a great practicum site for social work students interested in community resources, as it provides direct experience in connecting families to services, navigating systems, and supporting diverse populations. it builds key skills in cultural awareness and communication.
comments_supervisor: throughout my practicum at the family and community partnership office, claudia’s expertise has been invaluable in shaping my professional growth. her constructive feedback has refined my skills and encouraged new strategies for effective engagement. her mentorship has strengthened my commitment to providing high-quality service in my future practice.

most_helpful: the flexibility and staff openness to me shadowing, joining in, and learning from the variety of positions on site.
least_helpful: starting in the rcc position, especially in covering overnight and weekend shifts made me feel pretty separate from the other programming on site and learning opportunities.
recommend_reason: yes, definitely for a bsw. for an msw being really clear about opportunities to learn beyond the rcc position, encouraging the student to reach out to other staff members and facilitating those connections so that it becomes a more intentional learning space. also, a lot of my learning came from connecting mhm with class assignments. this helped deepen my learning and understanding.
comments_supervisor: group supervision was interesting and helpful, but i do feel like a lot of the time we stayed pretty surface level in the quality of that engagement. i'm not sure what would have helped us dive deeper, but more directly encouraging supervisees to bring their most challenging content and explicitly opening up space to process the feelings and being around that would have been helpful.

most_helpful: there were plenty of opportunities to learn about state and local agencies where head start could ask for training and get ideas about involvement when i attended conferences. head start was going to be reviewed by the office of head start and a couple of staff were trained in the class observation assessment and helped staff prepare for the review. i enjoyed attending school district meetings and helping the school district met staff evaluate students using the dayc 2 -the developmental assessment of young children. i learned a lot about their facilitation skills in conducting met and iep meetings.
least_helpful: pyramid model, the social emotional plan, and response to intervention were intervention plans i wanted to utilize to help support student learning and provide teacher support. i was not trained in how to write them and every time i asked if i was writing a plan correctly i was told i was but i still had lingering questions that were not answered. i researched the plans and went onto youtube to see how it was implemented. the plans were recommended to be used for anything, after doing my research that did not seem to be the case. i talked with my supervisor but due to other assignments needed to be done it was pushed aside. i think the intervention plans are good ideas if they are used correctly. i was not able to see one that was used successfully. there were 3 individuals in the office that approached me in how to conduct my job and job related tasks. i did not take their advice. there was one particular issue that i did not agree with which lead me to read navajo head start policies and procedures and standard of conduct to perform my job.
recommend_reason: i did not feel the support from my supervisor and there were many times i was not sure how to give my opinion without my supervisor turning into gossip or a type of unconstructive criticism.
other_comments: it seems that navajo head start is in a middle of a transition, the director has achievable goals and center staff (teachers, paraprofessionals, and bus drivers) need extra encouragement and hands on assistance to learn a new way of teaching their students and families. the administrative staff which included my position also need to be confident to support and use the tools available to us.

most_helpful: i was able to gain a much greater understanding of insurance driven care, how our community has a lack of available resources for our aging population,  our most vulnerable clients (mental health, homeless, teens) and how medical social work can work and not work.
least_helpful: i was allowed to do very little and i was only able to be at the hospital when my afi was there. it would have been very difficult to have gotten my hours if i had not been overly flexible with times and days which included weekends. other than my afi and a few staff members which included a couple of social workers, nurses, and one or two kind doctors it was an uncomfortable environment.
recommend_reason: the director of social workers did absolutely nothing to foster learning and actively prevented us from charting or shadowing other social workers.
comments_supervisor: i was in a small closet with my afi for 450 hours. i was very fortunate to have had mary who i professionally and personally got along well with. her experience and knowledge was very beneficial and i was fortunate to have been able to ask a lot of questions and have in-depth conversations.
other_comments: i feel fortunate to have been with mary. she worked to make the experience positive and she tried to create an environment where i could learn and grow. she helped me to zero in on my strengths and has helped me to pinpoint the area of social work that i am interested in.

most_helpful: the knowledge that i acquired included gaining insights into the legal process as well as identifying gaps within various communities. through my experiences, i was able to pinpoint the communities that possess the resources necessary to support the mental health community. additionally, i was able to establish many connections throughout the state and witness what a well-functioning team looks like. this allowed me to understand how supervisors can effectively support their employees, leading to greater success and the realization of their full potential. unfortunately, i also observed instances of poor media reporting and misinformation regarding the hospital. furthermore, it became evident to me how little state legislators understand about the hospital's inner workings, its management, and the reasons behind its operations.
least_helpful: each day was a constant and demanding grind, leaving me with scarce time to concentrate on the projects i was involved in.
recommend_reason: working at montana state hospital provides a glimpse into the areas of the mental health field that require improvement. it becomes apparent that there are insufficient resources to support individuals suffering from severe mental illnesses. additionally, community providers frequently decline to accept patients for various reasons. this experience highlights the level of dependence on the legal system, and how minor legislative changes can have far-reaching implications. while networking throughout the state, you encounter various individuals - some wonderful, others not so much, and families that are both grateful and hateful. you will experience it all
comments_supervisor: words cannot adequately express the gratitude i feel towards my afi, jackie evans. she has been instrumental in both challenging me and recognizing when i needed assistance or was overwhelmed. under her guidance, i have experienced significant growth and development. jackie's approach has made my short time at montana state hospital unforgettable and impactful. she not only provided me with the necessary flexibility, but also pushed me to realize my full potential. undoubtedly, jackie is one of the best supervisors i have ever had the privilege to work under.
other_comments: everyone should work at montana state hospital. you will be enlightened and motivated to bring change

most_helpful: they allowed me to be a counselor to the patients and implement my ideas into my work with them.
least_helpful: their system of social work is very antiquated and wasn’t beneficial in teaching me agency protocol.
recommend_reason: i had fantastic experiences helping patients and learned many things about what they need and what they don’t need.
comments_supervisor: jackie was helpful to me in every way she could even in learning spanish, culture, and how to understand political and agency policies.

most_helpful: my practicum at the nursing home was exceptionally educational because it immersed me in a compassionate older-adult community within a well-maintained facility. the unit was exceptionally clean and organized, and the staff consistently demonstrated kindness, patience, and efficiency. my afis were open to my questions, encouraged exploration, and provided abundant learning opportunities, which deepened my understanding of geriatric care and professional collaboration.
least_helpful: what i found least educational was the time spent being largely idle—completing homework rather than engaging directly with residents or clinical processes. at the nursing home, i wanted more hands-on interaction with older adults and exposure to resident documentation workflows. in the clinic setting, i wasn’t able to access or use the electronic health record system, nor connect with any patients in person, which limited my skill development in patient assessment and charting. going forward, i hope to find a practicum placement that better leverages my bsw training by offering structured opportunities to practice documentation and direct client engagement.
recommend_reason: within my learning goals, hands-on client engagement, exposure to documentation process, and use of electronic health record systems, i would not recommend this particular practicum site for another student with similar objectives. while the facility is well-run and the staff are kind, the placement did not offer sufficient opportunities for direct interaction with residents or practical training in charting and computer use. this may be due to a nursing staff environment, not social work training. for students seeking a practicum that fully utilizes their bsw skills, i would suggest a setting that combines client-facing responsibilities with a bit more structured instruction in documentation and electronics health records management, definitely!
comments_supervisor: excellent staff and entirely competent registered nurses!
other_comments: i’m very grateful for the opportunity to complete this practicum. it helped me establish a consistent schedule and brought much needed routine to my days, teaching me how to balance my academic responsibilities alongside practical work experience

recommend_reason: i felt i could manage this because i already had experience with addiction and was used to writing medical notes. i am older with life experience and had previous domestic violence experience. i was also well read on many psychology books independently and i am older with lots of life experience. this practicum could be completely overwhelming for someone without any strong base of support or life experience and you must have thick skin. these clients can be extremely hard.
comments_supervisor: my afi knows what she is doing and she will challenge you and your readiness to be your best. she is also great at pairing therapists with clients in a way that seems mutually beneficial and insightful.
other_comments: i vastly underestimated substance abuse. it is hard work with clients who don't want to be there so they can be extreme critics. this practicum allowed me the opportunity to work with vets, domestic violence, adhd and touch on trauma informed therapy and i greatly appreciated the experience.

recommend_reason: i think the agency does well with making sure that the learning agreement needs are met and that supervision hours are met weekly.

least_helpful: i would say that the professional development aspect was the least helpful for me. this is mainly because i was not involved with that committee as i wanted to focus on membership & outreach and policy development! it is hard to think of other things that were not helpful because nathan did a great job of catering to what i was interested in.
recommend_reason: i did recommend nasw mt to another student who ended up applying for their generalist practicum in 2025. they have not held the interviews yet, but i am eager to see if she gets it! i told her about the way nathan caters the experience to the students interests but also provides great insight and supervision that is reflective, honest, and helpful. i also told her that there is a small stipend, making it a very well-rounded practicum. i explained to her that it is a great space to learn about policy development (even without an active legislative session) as there is a lot of space to learn and a lot of work to do.
comments_supervisor: nathan is an amazing afi! he is very knowledgeable and honest with his feedback. he encourages transparency and takes the time to get to know the student, their learning style, how they receive feedback, and how they deal with conflict, etc. to make it the best experience possible. even though he and i were both remote, he was always present and available throughout the practicum.
other_comments: overall, it was a great experience. working a full-time job, part-time bartending job, going to school and doing practicum was difficult but doable due to nathan's flexibility and the flexibility of being remote. it would be helpful if more practicum placements offered a remote placement or a hybrid option. i think the practicum experiences in the program provide a lot of experience that we wouldn't get from a textbook, which i appreciate as a hands-on learner.

recommend_reason: overall this practicum placement is a wonderful experience. students will learn so so much, and will also have the ability to explore and dive deeper into their specific interests because of the variety of services offered. students gain experience in both micro and mezzo level practice (direct client work, and community/interagency work). the exposure gained to me is immeasurable. you gain exposure to several different realms of service, to many different agencies within the community, and are able to see different intersections such as how faith based organizations can work productively, ethically, and healthily in the community. specifically, the small staff at the welcome house offers a productive and healthy work environment. this can make a huge difference in the practicum experience. lara as an afi, is supportive and a great teacher, and is also open to learning herself. this makes for a very healthy team environment.
comments_supervisor: supervision is a huge strong suit of this organization. they set their employees and students up for success in how they engage with supervision and support.

agency_name: lewis and clark county detention center
agency_name_display: Lewis And Clark County Detention Center
most_helpful: my role was as an intern mental health therapist working in the l&c county detention center.  most helpful or educational: ~ the clinical experience of working 1:1 with inmates provided unique learning in determining what therapeutic needs they may have and how best to address them in context of a jail setting;~ provided opportunity to develop more therapeutic skills of presence, boundaries, and non-judgement.~ provided integrated view of carceral system with practice in identifying and engaging with the systemic challenges of the carceral system (i.e., racism, prevalence of substance use, mental health issues, unhoused status, and trauma -adverse childhood, ptsd, etc).  ~ working with personal ethical dilemmas that the setting and system presented.~ opportunities, when i, as an intern, served as the only presence of a "therapist" in the center prompting me to: engage more fully in interdisciplinary coordination(with staff, pre-release officers, medical staff, community resources, etc); determine ways to prioritize seeing clients while recognizing all can not be seen; practice pertinent self care.
least_helpful: everything was helpful and educational.  it was challenging.  experiencing what didn't work well within the agency was helpful and at time uncomfortable.
recommend_reason: pros: lots of opportunity for diverse learning: direct care for mental health or substance use; systemic/policy work; pre-release programming; case management; and parole work.cons; short staffing is a concern. agency is aware there may be times they can not serve as practicum site.
comments_supervisor: i had two afis, one onsite and a lcsw off site. both were invaluable. my onsite resigned in late winter, leaving me with no onsite therapist to consult with.  i appreciated the additional time with off-site lcsw - super helpful.
other_comments: this practicum site was challenging with significant responsibility.  my life experience provided me with key skills of presence, maturity, practicality, and interdisciplinary skills that allowed me to learn in the setting and, as well, contribute to the needs of a greatly understaffed department working with underserved clients.  we all agreed that this practicum site is for those that have some developed skills and perspectives.

recommend_reason: this is a great first year practicum agency for students interested in dabbling in both direct practice and micro level work. it is best suited for someone who is self-directed and has initiative to ask for what they need in terms of satisfying the goals of your learning agreement.

recommend_reason: i think someone who wants to go the micro clinical direction like i do in my professional job, would need to be prepared better to understand what a practicum like cryj would look like for their learning. i did not have a good grasp on what kind of learning i needed or wanted when i chose it as a practicum. shortly into the semester, i had a better understanding of what i really wanted to learn and did not feel cryj was the best option for me.  i think someone who is interested in mezzo or macro work would probably enjoy it better. someone who is interested in alternatives to the traditional justice system may have a better time working at a practicum like this.
comments_supervisor: my onsite afi  catherine was good. she provided guidance when needed, and answered my questions when i had them. i do think that andi was pivotal in making my practicum and more rich learning experience. andi was an amazing supervisor. they helped me so much. andi was inquisitive and helped me answer my own questions. they expanded my understanding of what it means to get the supervision needed to learn the most that i could. when i took the dynamic supervision class and the professor was talking about what makes a good supervisor, andi hit so many of the markers!i think the best part of the entire practicum process was working specifically with andi. i created multiple surveys and without andi's curiosity, i would have struggled to identify what learning i wanted to get from my practicum.
other_comments: overall i was pretty disappointed with the practicum process as a whole. i think it is important to encourage students to get the most dynamic learning experience possible with practicum, and due to the factors listed above i do not feel that i had the most dynamic experience i could have.

most_helpful: at my practicum agency, my afi was most helpful. all of his experiences gave me an understanding of what it is like to work in a hospital setting. he has taught me what it is like to advocate and serve clients. he explained the medical model and how it works in the hospital setting as we went to meetings and discussed patients and their needs. his trust enabled me to explore what i understood and ask questions as a practicum student.
least_helpful: what was least helpful was that the hospital was in the process of changing the role of a social worker in the hospital in 2024. i was no longer going into patient rooms and doing assessments. this also took time away from me in engaging as much as i was with families.
recommend_reason: there is a lot to offer to students who want to work in the medical field. this practicum offers a lot of practice for seeing and addressing change. a student will learn the medical model and how a social worker fits into that model. you will also learn to navigate well the role they will play as a social worker in the hospital and around the community.
comments_supervisor: stephen was great. we had a blast and he has a great sense of humor. what i appreciated the most was that he showed an interest in native american culture and what that meant to me as a person. he listened to the stories and even asked questions when he was unsure what something meant. i appreciated that about him.

most_helpful: being engaged with other people in the field. distance learning is very isolating and connecting with people again was a very important experience for me.
least_helpful: i don't have a comment about this.

comments_supervisor: lynn was a great afi and was able to help me refocus and make things work when the lack of structure and organization within the agency got in the way.

most_helpful: kayli is an amazing leader. even though she was supervising a large group of students she was able to respond quickly when things came up, and always made me feel heard and like i had a space to problem solve that was supported. she offered trainings the first semester before we started, as well as during our weekly meetings, and i really appreciated the opportunity to learn along the way. she encouraged us to work as a team and share ideas or ask questions of the other wscs, which was a big appeal of the program and a good way to learn.
least_helpful: the law school, where i was placed, is a tough environment to break into. kayli was helpful in brainstorming, but it is just a culture that is not very open to discussions of mental health and there was some hesitancy from students to meet. i think this will get better over time, and there are some faculty and staff who were very supportive of integrating this role this year and moving forward.
recommend_reason: i think the wsc role is a great one to grow and learn, as it can be pretty self directed but you always have support from kayli when things come up.
comments_supervisor: as i mentioned above, kayli was a great leader for the team. the role was pretty self directed and she allowed us space to determine what worked best for our specific placement, but i always knew there was support available if i needed it. she also took into account our feedback and requests for our weekly meetings and time together as a team. we had additional supervision with amy capalupo to fulfill the msw requirement, and i appreciated having multiple perspectives on issues as they arose and the ways their advice complemented one another.
other_comments: this was a great opportunity to meet with students and get to work on a variety of different skills - working with students as well as faculty and staff.

most_helpful: learning to work with clients cohesively
least_helpful: ongoing supports for workers within the agency
recommend_reason: i think it is very beneficial on the job practice and will help someone learn quickly.
comments_supervisor: i really enjoyed working with natalie

most_helpful: the braod variety of life situations and challenges experienced by the student body provided a rich learning opportunity for understanding the complex affects of poverty, addictions, abuse, neglect and trauma on young people, as well as the ways social workers can support them. it also gave me insight into the many limitations of school social workers. the team of social workers, behavior specailsts and teachers were extremely competent, innovative, professional and welcoming to me as a student and valued member of the team.
least_helpful: being a classroom based placement, there were significant barriers to my ability to engage in meaningful therapeutic support with students or their families. i spent a great deal of time observing the classroom and correcting behavior, which was not particularly educational or helpful. also the fact that the progrm was in its pilot year meant that my role was unclear and had to be created and changed as we went through the year.
recommend_reason: at this point it is unclear whether cali will continue next year, or if it does what changes will be made to its mission, goals and structure. until this is clarified i cannot recommend it. if the program continues, and if it can clearly define a role for a practicum student that ensures meaningful engagement , it could be a wonderful opportunity!
comments_supervisor: katie was a wonderful supervisor. she is very organized and professional, and took the role of supporting my learning competencies very seriously. given the limitations of the program, she did everything she could to support me having a rewarding and satisfying practicum experience. i highly recommend her to anyone looking for a school placement in the future!

least_helpful: i really can't think of anything that was least helpful. i have learned so much and was given so many opportunities for growth.
recommend_reason: the organization is really on their way to building a health organizational culture. they are consistently assessing and realigning things to ensure all employees feel safe and heard within the organization. while there is still work to be done to improve, they have done a great job and are receptive to feedback in ways that helps everyone from every walk of life.
comments_supervisor: tracie was always supportive in so many ways and she was a great person to bounce ideas off of. she gave great feedback and i knew i was able to go to her when something was bothering me.
other_comments: while my practicum experience was mostly positive, there were a few moments where it became difficult in terms of being an indigenous person. in the very beginning of my generalist year, i was at a different site and experienced some situations that were unexpectedly traumatizing for me because of my identity. indigenous children are over-represented in the foster care system and to help future students know what to expect, maybe create some kind of awareness training and/or scenario when things arise (like a white foster family cutting an indigenous child's hair without their birth parent's consent) with ethical dilemmas. i wasn't prepared for how that would impact me and it ultimately led me to switch sites because other similar instances added to that first experience. another is to help students navigate who their msw supervisor is and ensuring they are choosing wisely. perhaps parameters in who is allowed to provide msw supervisor.

most_helpful: the variety of experiences provided was the most helpful/educational part of the experience. it was terrific to have both the group and individual therapy experience.
least_helpful: lack of time by anyone in a supervising role. it was probably not a great idea for them to take me on as a practicum student given how over committed all the treatment staff are.  also the residential program had terrific flexibility in the first half of my practicum so i was able to build appropriate programming for ptsd groups - but then it adopted a rigid worksheet type model for most groups after january which made it hard to add much of substance to the topics
recommend_reason: it requires a lot of autonomy and confidence. the program is not set up for msw students - it is far more oriented for psych grad students and nurse practitioners and more medically oriented roles.  it appears that lcsw's are not valued as much as psych practictioners and there is a clear division that looks like there is a hierarchy/arrogance at play with the psych practitioners. i feel it is not warranted - usually these people are much more challenged in building simple rapport with others. weird environment.  any msw student needs to let this roll off their backs - requires certain way of looking at the world and maturity.
other_comments: it was terrific to have the individual therapy experience.

most_helpful: first step: the supervision and overall support of the entire staff helped me feel welcome and comfortable, to be a learner, ask for help, and to deal with secondary traumatic stress of the work.sarc: the opportunity to practice counseling 1-on-1 with um students and receive clinical supervision from andi was most educational. i also found group supervision to be a helpful forum to learn from peers in the clinical psychology and counseling programs, and to ask my own questions about my work.
least_helpful: first step: i am not sure i could identify anything at all that was not helpful, to be honest. i feel immensely grateful and lucky to have had a 2-year placement here.sarc: i did not have the clinical support i needed with sarc supervision alone; i was able to get extra support through the msw program's allowing me to get outside supervision with andi, which i valued so much. also, my afi and our director were new, and everyone was learning new systems. as a result i did not get adequate training. lastly, there were times when i felt i had to take greater responsibility than i thought i was ready for and needed more guidance and support.
recommend_reason: first step: students have the opportunity to do deeper learning in a 2-year placement. there are multiple systems that students will learn about and interact with, including cfs, law enforcement, and the county attorney's office, to name a few. lastly, first step invited me to staff meetings, case review, trauma support, and other events and treated me like any other member of the team.sarc: i would recommend this placement to a self-motivated student who is able to identify their own learning goals and work toward them within and apart from the placement itself. a student who is able to do the above, as well as set healthy boundaries, ask for help, and keep themselves healthy, would gain clinical experience that is not offered by many other placements.
comments_supervisor: i can't think of any additional comments that i haven't already mentioned.
other_comments: both of my practicums were paid, which was the thing that allowed me to do them. financially, i needed to be working at least 20-30 hours/week to make rent and pay bills, etc, even with taking out ~$60k in student loans. i am so grateful that both of my field placements offered me pay, and i know that many other placements did not offer pay for their practicum students. i would advocate that the program better prioritize pay equity in all practicum experiences in order to support students of all financial abilities during the program.

most_helpful: i think having a team ou+g3:g241tside of my practicum agency was most helpful. the team listened to and carefully considered the issues the msw intern students. for me, it was beneficial knowing that i belonged to something larger than my practicum.
least_helpful: there were many things at my practicum agency that were not helpful or educational and although i have chosen to use the experience in the most positive light as possible, i cannot help but to think of the laundry list of topics i would not want another msw to experience.
recommend_reason: during my time at the agency, it became evident that little consideration was given to employees' physical or mental well-being. this was reflected in nearly all interactions within the organization. one of the most significant issues was the lack of efficient training, which left me struggling to understand my job responsibilities—particularly regarding transportation, as outlined in the job description.discrepancies in office policies created inconsistency, with many decisions left to individual case managers, leading to confusion and billing concerns. the employee manual required case managers to talk on the phone and take notes while driving. when safety concerns were raised about this policy, they were quickly dismissed. additionally, my office was located in a separate building in a basement, and any voiced safety concerns were disregarded.the agency’s disregard for fair labor practices was also concerning. on one occasion, the owner requested that i complete a task but later adjusted my timecard because medicaid could not be billed for my work—essentially requiring me to work for free. case managers were also expected to attend unpaid potluck lunches. when i requested a heater for my office space, i was told that case managers were not responsible enough and might burn down the building, despite other offices having heaters.regarding client care, i witnessed unethical financial practices. the agency provides payee services and charged one of my clients for a room in their group home, even though there was no available space. after prepaying, the client was later informed he was no longer eligible for the group home. when i advocated for a full refund, my concerns were dismissed, and it remains unclear how much, if any, of his money was returned.more recently, the executive director prematurely moved a client off my caseload and reassigned them to a new case manager. when the client inquired about the change, the executive director falsely claimed that i had requested the transfer. the client later left a voicemail expressing deep hurt over what she was told i had said about her. this unfortunate and unethical decision retraumatized the client and could have lasting negative effects.additionally, one of the doctors on staff had previously been reprimanded in another state and lost his license due to an inappropriate relationship with a client at another mental health facility. despite this serious ethical violation, he was still employed at the agency, raising concerns about the organization’s hiring and oversight practices.if i learned anything from this agency, it would be how to remain ethical in practice while working in an unethical environment. navigating these challenges reinforced the importance of integrity, advocacy, and adherence to ethical standards, even when those around me failed to uphold them.these are just a few examples of the agency's problematic practices, and unfortunately, there are many more. given my experience, i would not recommend this practicum site to another msw student.
comments_supervisor: my afi created an environment that made my practicum bearable.
other_comments: i hope you will remove 3 rivers mental health solutions as a practicum site.

most_helpful: having the opportunity to build relationships and connections with participants. also, having the opportunity to engage in some macro-level work by participating in the racial justice taskforce. building relationships with peers based off solidarity in the harmfulness of this agency and working together to challenge the workplace culture.
least_helpful: unethical practices and procedures, racism within the agency (against staff & participants), the unethical preparedness of clinical interns (minimal training, limited and chronically changing / unstable supervision, what i would remark as 'too much independence' for the lack of a better term regarding autonomy engaging in clinical interventions that perhaps one isn't ready / qualified to engage in), obstructive policies regarding direct service staff's ability to enact change or give insight to harmful policies or practices occurring within shelter, retaliation from my afi when voicing various concerns and experiences of racism (she filled out my end of the year evaluation early, in the beginning of march, and scored me deceptively low, the day after i attempted to voice my concerns), unethical supervisory relationships between subordinates and supervisors (several supervisors were having sexual relationships with their inferiors, and one even attempted to cultivate an external relationship with myself, which made me blatantly uncomfortable, and was somewhat traumatizing as a young afab individual in a position of vulnerability, and was never rectified), illegally biased and targeted practices regarding reporting instances of child abuse and neglect, discrimination and non-ada compliance perpetuated against direct service staff by directors and managers whom hold minimal relationships or contact with said participants, being an indigenous student and being forced to sideline myself which indigenous participants were experiencing racism and harm at the hands of white staff in positions of power, (i.e., a housing director calling the police on an eight year old indigenous child who was experiencing mental health crisis and displayed absolutely no violent behavior or actions to others, which resulted in them being handcuffed in our parking lot, undoubtedly traumatizing them for the rest of their life), experiencing ageism, and being subjected to retaliation and vindictive communication on a regular basis towards the end of my practicum placement, again, more than likely a result of racism, homophobia, or ageism.
recommend_reason: absolutely not. as a social worker and also as someone who intersectionally holds several disenfranchised identities (and some privileged ones as well), i would never want another student to experience the same experience i did. this placement i will say taught me many things, some supportive, such as what i learned from trauma-conscious peers or by cultivating meaningful relationships with participants, however, overall, this experience was again, extremely negatively impactful on my general well-being, which i am now taking time to recover and recuperate from. additionally, the stress & aggressions related to this work environment greatly impacted my capacity to engage in schoolwork & courses. i managed to maintain good grades; however, i feel as though i would have been more present within classes had i not been subjected to this environment. i would not ask anyone paying for a degree & internship that is implemented in order to support one's learning and growth as both an individual & future social work / professional level, be subjected to the same experience that i was. i deserved better, and so do my classmates, especially those who hold marginalized identities. i think this placement is emotionally, spiritually and intellectually dangerous to place new social workers in. i was fortunate enough to have a foundational background in social worker, which i'm sure aided in my insights that were painful, however the privilege of coming from this educational background was also supportive and empowering in my awareness of the shortcomings of this agency, and in many ways 'kept me safe'. i know that many individuals in our program are not coming from an undergraduate bsw education, and i fear that this agency will exploit those blind spots, as i was reprimanded and retaliated against when i did call attention to discrepancies, unethical procedures, etc.
comments_supervisor: carol roberts has shown to have an extremely biased approach to interactions with clinical interns. initially, our relationship and dynamic was positive and supportive, she then made continual microaggressions against me, despite me trying to advocate for myself, and as i became more outspoken regarding the harm this agency inflicts, the more frustrated carol become with me. this is documented on my behalf through email chains, sonia evaluations, and with peer verification. she did not evaluate my work within the agency in a way that was accurate, comprehensive or acknowledged all of my efforts, despite me objectively completing a wider variety of tasks and projects than my peer interns who were older, white cisgendered women (other than 1 intern, whom's participation was also affected by the racism within this agency who i will not speak for). carol also held a meeting with me, inquired about my resistance, frustration, and criticisms, once i voiced such, i was met with defensiveness & dismissal, which then incited an abundance of anxiety and fear, regarding this power dynamic & how traumatizing it was to be in this agency. this meeting was by far, the worst meeting i have ever been a part of. i have worked in many social service agencies, even their sister agency sarc, and have never received such feedback or been met with such hostility, especially from someone who is a therapeutic provider. i'm egregiously disappointed that this individual is entrusted with the well-being and development of several new counselors in the community, especially those who are providing services for clients in acute, chronic, and severe, trauma. aside from experiencing retributions for what i've been asked to do as a social work student (critically examine, reflect upon, and advocate for positive change that is anti-racist, anti-colonial, and ethical), the clinical program is also not comprehensive or trauma-informed within itself, which i can ascertain is harmful to both participants and interns. supervision was not regular (i additionally have documentation of 7 times in which the process of how to schedule supervision was changed, as well as carol requesting to move my supervision several times), training is insufficient, and interns are expected to have an abundance of clinical leadership within their roles. i also was informed i would have several shadow opportunities (for first step shifts, as well as therapy intake appointments), and neither were offered, and i was expected to take on two counseling clients despite my constant communication about how i was uncomfortable and felt underprepared. i am still anxious and dealing with the aftereffects of how compromising my own values & intentions for practice both made me feel as a human being and as a social worker, and worried for my clients and how they were impacted as well. this is outright traumatizing to new social workers. there is a difference between being nervous and prepared and being thrown into the ocean and expected to swim. i only received maybe 4 supervision meetings for the entirety of my second half of my placement (one was when carol was aggressive towards me), meaning i was administering counseling independently. these practices are unethical and illegal. additionally, carol claimed i was not 'seeking guidance' from other peers, which she suggested i engage in as a surrogate form of dialogue to take place of our supervision meetings that weren't occurring (also unethical to ask other interns to take ownership of mentoring me, as well as insinuate i wasn't seeking support, as it is clearly and undeniably documented this is false), and i did. i spoke with hannah lohmuller several times regarding my many concerns, as she can corroborate, as well as tracy, jen, and my other supervisor, gage weston. i went to excessive and extraordinary to incorporate external perspectives and obtain ethical standards of supervision, despite how i was evaluated regarding said efforts. i also have email documentation of such.
other_comments: this needs to be rectified. if students continue at this placement this year, i will be gravely disappointed, and more so disturbed at the inadequacy of the consideration of due diligence, to participants and ssw students.

least_helpful: i think there were times where i felt like i had no clear direction about what to do. we are working on getting a drop-in center up and running and when we started i was confused. however, when i was able to sit down and dig into the paperwork i was able to formulate questions to ask my afi that helped make the directive clearer. this helped us get things progressing forward.
recommend_reason: i would not recommend my current practicum placement to other students because mid way through we ended up losing the crisis response team. however, there still is a care coordinator. there are simply too many unknowns at this agency right now. it does not seem like a stable learning environment, unfortunately. without the crt, my afi is only doing her probation job. which i did learn a lot of valuable skills from, but it is not ideal for a social work student.
comments_supervisor: i learned many valuable skills from my afi, she was great. she demonstrated true ethical social work and she showed her clients that she genuinely cared and wanted the best for them. this was very inspiring to me because she is a probation office and many times we see probation being very ridged in their work. i told her that i see that she genuinely cares and that i look up to her for that.

most_helpful: there were many things about my practicum agency that were helpful and educational including being able to observe the entire criminal justice system from law enforcement, to court, to social services; being able to participate in trainings that furthered my educational experience; and being able to communicate with my supervisor about questions, comments, and work experiences.
least_helpful: in my practicum agency, everything that i participated in was both helpful and educational. everything that i participated in allowed me to improve my knowledge and/or skills.
recommend_reason: i would recommend this practicum agency to another student because it is able to fuel learning through constant opportunities, experiences, and education. it is an extremely informative practicum that will allow an individual to take conceptual knowledge and apply it to real world situations.
comments_supervisor: the use of supervision in my practicum has been extremely beneficial in improving my educational experience. when an individual has a professional, proactive, experienced, and communicative afi as i did, the comprehension of social work competencies, skills, and knowledge is greatly benefited. in addition, the supervision allows for comments, questions, and concerns to be raised and answered.
other_comments: this practicum experience was a tremendous opportunity. it allowed me to gain knowledge, skills, and experience needed when working in a social work capacity.

other_comments: i am very thankful for the ability to do a work based placement. it has enabled me to be successful through college.

most_helpful: there were several components of my practicum experince that i will take with me into my career. for one, i participated in the coordinated care team meetings with other care professionals on campus. this helped me gain a better understanding of the campus culture and common barriers for student success, as well as see a variety of social workers and their approaches. while the bear necessities team is limited, i got a variety of perspectives through coordinated care. additionally, i loved working in the food pantry. partnering the food pantry with some case management gave a great blend of micro/mezzo work, and i learned more about our clients by working in the food pantry. finally, and most important, my afi was a huge part of my success at bear necessities. kat really took the time every single day to teach me, and would often take twice as long on a task just so i could understand it. kat really is an exceptional mentor and has put in a lot of work to make the bear necessities work environment healthy, safe and fun.
least_helpful: there were times throughout the year where some miscommunications between myself and kat would take place, leading to some minor conflict. by the midyear mark, we had worked through the majority of these issues and i got better at communicated when i was unsure, and kat got better at clarifying.
recommend_reason: i would absolutely recommend this practicum to other students! kat is an amazing supervisor and bear necessities offered me a wide variety of experiences and knowledge!
comments_supervisor: kat was the best supervisor i have ever had! they were extremely supportive, kind and understanding,.
other_comments: thank you guys! :)

most_helpful: working out tough scenarios in real time and not just discussing them in a classroom setting.
least_helpful: i feel like there was not a lot of opportunity for me in the advocacy realm, which is hard to achieve at an afterschool program.
recommend_reason: i loved this experience! bgcm cares so much about child development, attachment theory, and trauma informed practices. i have learned so much by actually working with children, and it has made me value them even more than i already did. plus the work environment is awesome and fosters so much growth.
comments_supervisor: loved my afi and she gave me some seriously helpful advice on some of my most troubling practice dilemmas!
other_comments: i again couldn't be more happy with my choice. this practicum has honestly changed my perspective on life and also helped me figure out what i want to do for my future career and the population i want to work with. i never thought i would feel so fulfilled from this, so i am so glad.

most_helpful: rapport-building was one of the most helpful parts of the placement because it shaped every other intervention skill. i could see how assessment, intervention, and evaluation work together.
least_helpful: the documentation burden could be heavy and sometimes limited opportunities for direct work.
recommend_reason: the agency offered some good exposure, but i would hesitate to recommend it broadly because supervision and feedback were variable. the main concerns were relapse complexity, documentation burden.

most_helpful: i found care planning very helpful because it linked assessment to realistic intervention steps. it also reinforced a person-in-environment perspective.
least_helpful: there was strong case management learning, but less policy exposure than i expected.
recommend_reason: i would recommend this placement to another student because the supervision, learning climate, and exposure to social work roles were all strong. the strongest areas were family systems, community resources.

most_helpful: i found the empowerment model very helpful because it focused on client strengths and capacity building.
least_helpful: on-call demands were educational but also one of the more difficult aspects of placement.
recommend_reason: yes. the agency provided helpful supervision, opportunities for direct practice, and a clear connection between theory and practice. the strongest areas were advocacy, safety planning.
comments_supervisor: my afi provided reflective supervision and helped me think critically about assessment, intervention, and documentation.

most_helpful: i learned a lot about resource coordination and how to connect needs, services, and follow-up.
least_helpful: the least helpful part was having fewer opportunities for direct service than expected.
recommend_reason: yes, i would recommend this agency because i gained practical experience in assessment, intervention, and professional development with consistent support. the strongest areas were social justice, advocacy.
comments_supervisor: i would have benefited from more consistent supervision and clearer feedback on my performance.

most_helpful: case management and resource linkage were especially helpful because they showed me how social work operates across systems. it also reinforced a person-in-environment perspective.

recommend_reason: i would recommend this placement to another student because the supervision, learning climate, and exposure to social work roles were all strong. the strongest areas were brief intervention, interdisciplinary team.

most_helpful: the supervision was excellent; i received clear feedback, support with ethical dilemmas, and space to reflect on practice. it strengthened my understanding of generalist practice across systems.

recommend_reason: yes. the agency provided helpful supervision, opportunities for direct practice, and a clear connection between theory and practice. the strongest areas were assessment, ethical decision-making.

most_helpful: the team-based environment was very helpful because i could see assessment, discharge planning, and intervention from multiple perspectives. it connected well with social justice and self-determination.

recommend_reason: yes. the agency provided helpful supervision, opportunities for direct practice, and a clear connection between theory and practice. the strongest areas were documentation, discharge planning.
