recommend_reason: this practicum location provided a culture that is caring and compassionate. family health and wellness encourages open and honest communication between learners, mentors, and staff for addressing concerns and fosters a collaborative environment. additionally, fhw embraces diversity and values different perspectives and backgrounds. mentors and staff model professional behavior and expectations by providing clear learning objectives, expectations, and assessment methods. fhw promotes a culture of continuous learning and improvement fostering a growth mindset.
comments_supervisor: kayla is the true embodiment of social work values and principles and her exceptional mentorship has been instrumental in my growth as a social worker. she consistently demonstrates the ideals of social work in her daily interactions and has had a profound and positive impact on the lives of those she serves. her deep understanding of the social work field and her willingness to share her knowledge and experience has been invaluable to my professional growth. she demonstrates a strong commitment to anti-racism, equity, diversity and inclusion in their agency work and social work practice. kayla is a compassionate and empathetic mentor who has provided constructive and actionable feedback that's helped me to improve my skills and knowledge. she fosters a supportive and collaborative environment that makes me feel valued and respected which has helped me to develop my confidence and self-belief. kayla consistently goes above and beyond to support my learning and well-being while inspiring me to reach my full potential through her own actions and encouragement. she is a true leader and mentor and i am so grateful for her guidance.

most_helpful: one of the most helpful and educational aspects of my practicum agency was seeing how my work intersects with systemic barriers in real time. i worked in a school setting, so while i was supporting students directly, i was also constantly navigating gaps in services, long waitlists for mental health care, and policy constraints around discipline and funding. that context pushed me to think beyond individual interventions and consider the broader systems students are up against.i also learned a lot from the multidisciplinary team—especially how each person brought a different lens to problem-solving. watching how a school counselor, an admin, and a social worker might approach the same situation differently helped me understand the importance of collaborative, values-driven communication.
least_helpful: one thing that made the work harder—and more heartbreaking—was knowing that some of our students really needed more than the school could provide. whether it was long waitlists for mental health care, a lack of family support, or no clear path for long-term services, i often felt like we were holding a lot with very little. that experience sharpened my understanding of how systems fail students—and strengthened my commitment to advocacy and trauma-informed care.
recommend_reason: it’s a setting where you get to build deep, ongoing relationships with students, see how therapeutic and academic goals can support one another, and practice flexible, student-centered approaches every day.if you’re someone who thrives in a fast-paced, hands-on environment and wants to learn how to support students with complex needs in real-world ways, this practicum offers that. it’s also an opportunity to see how systemic barriers show up in daily school life, and how to advocate for students within those limitations. it’s not always easy—especially given the resource gaps—but it’s incredibly meaningful work that leaves you with a clearer sense of the kind of social worker and educator you want to be.
comments_supervisor: chad berg was a consistent and grounding presence throughout my practicum. he created space for honest reflection, encouraged critical thinking, and modeled how to navigate complex student and system-level dynamics with both compassion and clarity. his style of supervision was approachable and thoughtful—he didn’t rush to give answers, but instead asked questions that helped me see things from multiple angles and consider the broader implications of my work.given the nature of my practicum—where i was also the classroom teacher— the quality of supervision time made a lasting impact. chad’s insights helped me slow down, name what i was feeling, and connect the day-to-day work to broader social work values. i especially appreciated his ability to hold both the emotional realities of this work and the structural factors at play. his supervision helped me grow not just in skills, but in confidence and clarity about my role as a social worker.
other_comments: my experience with outside group supervision varied across the two years. during my first year, the group felt more taxing than supportive. at a time when i was already navigating a complex practicum and heavy responsibilities, it was difficult to also hold space for peers who were just beginning to engage with the foundational aspects of this work. the group lacked cohesion and direction, which made it hard to feel grounded or get what i needed from the experience.in contrast, my second-year group supervision was much more helpful and meaningful. the facilitator brought a high level of skill, structure, and insight, and the group dynamic—with more experienced classmates, even among some first-year practicum students—led to richer conversations and deeper reflection. that combination made a significant difference in helping me feel supported and challenged in the ways i needed as i moved toward the end of the msw program.

most_helpful: one of the most helpful and educational aspects of my practicum at watson children’s shelter was the opportunity to work directly with children and families in a trauma-informed setting. i gained hands-on experience applying social work theories and interventions in real-world situations. additionally, having barb as my supervisor was incredibly impactful her mentorship, feedback, and support helped me grow in confidence and deepen my understanding of generalist practice.
least_helpful: na
comments_supervisor: barb is the best!

most_helpful: one of the most helpful aspects of my practicum agency was my supervisor's willingness to clarify doubts and openly share her knowledge. her guidance made complex concepts more approachable and deepened my understanding of practical applications in the field.
least_helpful: honestly, everything about my practicum agency was positive. the environment was positive, the learning opportunities were enriching, and my supervisor was always willing to help me understand my role as a future sw. i never felt like there was a lack of educational value, every experience contributed to my professional growth in some way. it was truly a well-rounded and rewarding practicum.
recommend_reason: absolutely! i would highly recommend my practicum agency to another student with similar learning and career goals. the agency provided a supportive and enriching environment, where learning was encouraged and practical experience was readily available. my supervisor was instrumental in clarifying doubts and asking questions about complex situations, making difficult concepts more approachable. the agency also aligned well with the learning agreement, offering opportunities for professional development, ethical decision-making, and engagement in diversity, equity, and inclusion practices. every aspect of my practicum experience contributed positively to my growth, making it an ideal setting for students seeking a comprehensive and rewarding learning experience.
comments_supervisor: i greatly appreciated the guidance and support provided by my afi. their commitment to supervision fostered a positive learning experience, ensuring i felt well-prepared and confident in my practicum responsibilities.

most_helpful: one of the most helpful parts of my practicum at aware inc. was being able to work directly with youth in a group home setting and really see how trauma-informed care plays out in everyday situations. it helped me connect theory to practice in a real and meaningful way. working alongside an interdisciplinary team was also incredibly educational—i learned so much by seeing different perspectives come together to support each youth. another huge part of my learning came from my afi, who took the time to walk me through the behind-the-scenes process of intake review and clinical assessments before a youth is even accepted into the home. that experience really sharpened my understanding of how the full process of helping youth unfolds, from initial assessment all the way through ongoing care. supervision throughout the year also gave me a space to reflect, ask questions, and keep building my confidence as a social work professional.
least_helpful: while my practicum at aware inc. was overall a great learning experience, there were times when the fast-paced environment made it harder to slow down and get in-depth learning around certain clinical processes. because direct care is the priority in the group home setting, there were fewer opportunities for formal clinical skill-building compared to what might happen in a more traditional outpatient setting. however, i still gained valuable experience through observation, supervision, and hands-on practice, and i appreciate the efforts my afi made to bridge that gap when possible.
recommend_reason: yes, i would recommend aware inc. to another student with similar learning and career goals. working in the group home setting gave me real, hands-on experience with youth who have complex needs, and it helped me build a strong foundation in trauma-informed care, crisis response, and interdisciplinary teamwork. while it’s a fast-paced environment that requires flexibility, it’s a great fit for students who want to strengthen their direct practice skills, learn how systems impact youth, and get a real-world view of mental health and child welfare services. plus, with the right support and supervision, there are a lot of opportunities to grow your clinical understanding alongside your direct care skills.
comments_supervisor: i’m really grateful for the support and guidance i received from my afi throughout my practicum. she took the time to not only provide supervision around day-to-day situations but also to connect me to the bigger picture—helping me understand clinical assessments, intake processes, and how decisions are made before a youth even enters care. our supervision sessions were a valuable space for reflection, learning, and growth, and they made a big difference in helping me feel more confident and prepared for the next steps in my social work career.
other_comments: overall, my practicum experience at aware inc. gave me a solid foundation for my future work in social work and mental health. it wasn’t always easy, but the challenges pushed me to grow both personally and professionally. i’m grateful for the hands-on experience, the support from my afi and team, and the chance to really connect theory to practice. this year helped me build confidence, deepen my understanding of trauma-informed care, and feel more prepared for my specialization year and beyond.

most_helpful: participating in program development and evaluation at a mezzo level. implementing trauma informed care and evidence based practices. the experience of developing practices based on peer reviewed literature.
least_helpful: i don't recall a single experience that i did not learn something from. i was even able to learn from experiences that involved conflict among leadership of other agencies and how to better manager conflict in the future.
recommend_reason: crisis connection is involved in all aspects of crisis. students have direct exposure to crisis and suicide interventions for adults and children, working with emergency departments, psychiatric hospitals, first responders, jails, families, and the laws related to involuntary treatment.
comments_supervisor: my practicum agency has a practicum manager position dedicated to the role of afi which makes supervision easily accessible.

most_helpful: practicing clinical work with strong supervision in a supportive environment.
least_helpful: this is hard to say. the learning experience was so rich! i think if anything, school didn't support me in the practicum because the classes were leadership/policy and i was doing intense 1 on 1 clinical work.
recommend_reason: amazing experience to learn/practice clinical work in a supportive setting. have to feel ready/confident enough to dive into seeing up to 12 clients per week, but such rich learning and supervision.
comments_supervisor: i enjoyed having 2 supervisors and group supervision. so much support and ability to pop your head into their office whenever.
other_comments: i hope social work students will continue to have the opportunity to be a clinical intern at curry! with how many classmates want to start clinical work post-grad, i think there should be way more opportunities like this. the lack of clinical practicum placements is a disservice to students and the clients we will serve after school.

most_helpful: seeing how social work practice impacts outcomes with children and families, and how focusing on helping families identify what their strengths and needs are has been meaningful. learning more about the policies and procedures affecting decisions made during the life of cases is helpful as well.
least_helpful: i think it would have been more helpful to be able to receive more training in certain areas for my new position that were really focused on allowing me to input my individual style and knowledge into the position rather than the expectations of things being done the same way others are doing things.  we are all individuals and have individual styles that work for us, and that flexibility is necessary to reach one's full potential in any position i feel.
recommend_reason: child welfare is a complex field with many different components, but the core competencies can be seen and met in a reasonable period of time, with a variety of experiences.
comments_supervisor: my supervisor is incredibly busy with a lot of duties as child welfare manager. he is very knowledgable in all policies, procedures and other areas of the agency as someone who has worked here for a very long time. he is always willing to help and answer any questions, but is stretched for time with his own duties as child welfare manager and also having youth on his caseload which makes it difficult to always be available for supervision questions.

most_helpful: shadowing the intake coordinators within the community. i was able to complete intakes with clients and advocate and work with them around receiving mental health services. i was also able to see intake coordinators utilize behavioral therapy techniques to assist with the intake process.
least_helpful: communication was slightly difficult due to not being on the agency site myself. it caused some miscommunication on my end and moved things slower.
recommend_reason: there is a great source of networking with other agencies that take place and staff is very knowledgable around behavioral health interventions. working with vulnerable populations is also helpful when receiving experience, because it is where we can work through more intense barriers and challenges.

most_helpful: supervision
least_helpful: nothing
recommend_reason: youth homes is a great organization and a i have felt very supported.
comments_supervisor: charlie has been very supportive and helpful.

most_helpful: supportive supervision, collaboration with other social workers and providers, opportunity to practice clinical skills and client engagement
least_helpful: unpredictable clinic schedule - some days were very slow
recommend_reason: good clinical practice, fantastic supervision support
comments_supervisor: felt very well supported.

most_helpful: the aspects of my practicum that were most helpful were the clinical experience i received, the training in mental health, and, overall, the knowledge i gained about eating disorders.
least_helpful: every aspect of the experience was helpful in some way, and i cannot think of anything that was least helpful.
recommend_reason: i would recommend it to another student as it was an educational, productive, and relevant practicum.
comments_supervisor: my afi experience was beneficial to me. we met frequently, and i learned a great deal from them.

most_helpful: the community connections and years of experience held by the field supervisor
least_helpful: limited exposure to bureaucracy and government procedures/grant application
recommend_reason: the opportunity to create my own practicum experience in conjunction with my field supervisor allowed a great deal of independence, self-sufficiency and drive. it encouraged me to seek out opportunities, trainings and networking opportunities that have set me up to succeed in my community after graduation, beyond just educational aspects.
other_comments: i believe there is some drastic inequity in practicum requirements - many students were able to complete a paid practicum, and most of us who didn't still had to work full-time, as well as the additional 16 hours/week. this additional strain was significant. i would strongly recommend everyone be held to the same standards, and employment-based practicums should not be approved unless it is an additional 16 hours per week outside of their regular employment.

agency_name: alliance for suicide prevention of larimer county
agency_name_display: Alliance For Suicide Prevention Of Larimer County
most_helpful: the guidance from my afi and helping out with presentations/powerpoints. i learned a lot as i reviewed the materials and contributed with updated research/info.
least_helpful: nothing! i learned something from all of my various tasks.
recommend_reason: the support, flexibility, and team collaboration was so valuable. asp cares for the staff and interns just as much as they care for their community.

most_helpful: the hands on learning and a field instructor who took time to help me in a new position. she had patience with me and sent me in different directions to observe others and see how i could do things better.
least_helpful: i cannot think of anything that was the least helpful or educational. i think what we put into our practicum is what we will get out of it. i went into this practicum wanting to learn all there was and i think i learned a lot because of that attitude.
recommend_reason: fair-mont-egan school is a great place to get hands on experience and connection with the students. i did not realize all the areas i could learn in and i am so thankful i did my practicum here.
comments_supervisor: brandy has a lot of experince in education and she wants to create leaders in her staff. in her own words she is always looking to "build your resume". while that can be intimidating in the moment, i look back on these two years and see that i would not be where i am today without her. she showed patience, wisdom, and grace and i am so thankful to have gotten to work under her.

agency_name: chippewa cree tribe human services division
agency_name_display: Chippewa Cree Tribe Human Services Division
most_helpful: the hands-on experience to apply theories, interventions, and skills for motivational interviewing, trauma-informed care, etc.
least_helpful: a lack of structure or clear expectations, training is limited, and there is no guidance on how to navigate client work confidently.

most_helpful: the intersection of micro, mezzo, and macro level social work at mrwh has been incredibly instructive. throughout this practicum, i had the opportunity to thoughtfully experiment with different therapeutic modalities while building relationships with clients over the course of the year. in my work with clients, i also had the opportunity to interact with the kinds of systems *they* interact with, including supportive housing, ssi/ssdi, first responders/emergency care, and medicaid. at the organizational level, i was able to participate in treatment plan development/updates with clients and the rest of their team as well as develop a deeper understanding of the full landscape of services (and service gaps) that exist for individuals with sdmi. the latter included touring the local detention center and the montana state hospital, two locations that many of our clients have experience with.
least_helpful: it was certainly a different experience to not have jen on site for the second semester of my practicum (both for me and particularly for the agency). some of the organizational struggle that has been present in the last few months at mrwh has had a direct impact on clients, which was challenging to navigate therapeutically. on the other than, much of that agency dysfunction felt instructive in its own way.
recommend_reason: i absolutely think there will be a point in the future where this would be a good practicum site again, but as jen's clinical position hasn't been filled within the residential program, a new practicum wouldn't have the kind of onsite clinical supervision that i benefited from my first few months.
comments_supervisor: jen preble is a fantastic supervisor, and i would strongly recommend practicum opportunities that she is a part of. she went out of her way to make sure that i had no loss in access to her after she moved and her support/mentorship was instrumental in how i handled clinical encounters.

most_helpful: the intersectionality of the criminal justice system, houselessness, substance use, trauma, and policy.
least_helpful: i was not fully prepared to step into the role of an aclc, but that was due to a lack in my own education and understanding.
recommend_reason: incredibly dynamic place to work and learn. great opportunity to see how policy affects clients directly and in direct practice.
comments_supervisor: kaylee has a lot of experience and was always willing to talk with me or help me problem solve, even outside supervision hours.

most_helpful: the level of support from afi, and other full time staff was instrumental and educational in the application of learning and implementation and understanding of the practice.
least_helpful: cys offered many great things. i cannot really think of a "least" helpful or educational aspect of cys regarding my practicum experience.
recommend_reason: tremendous culture and support.
comments_supervisor: jeremy is/was a great asset in helping me navigate work with clients as well as understanding processes in real world thinking and preparation with clients.
other_comments: i highly recommend using california youth services in the future if there are any students in the los angeles/orange county area.

most_helpful: the most helpful and educational aspect of my practicum at the iris clubhouse was gaining a deeper understanding of how a nonprofit truly functions behind the scenes. being guided through the complexities of fundraising, outreach, and capacity building only enhanced my appreciation for this work. the director’s leadership was especially impactful, her commitment to member voice, ethical decision-making, and community inclusion set the tone for everything i was able to learn and contribute.
least_helpful: the least helpful aspect of my practicum was the limited opportunity for clinical experiences. while the iris clubhouse offered a strong foundation in community-based, recovery-oriented work, the setting is not clinical in nature, which meant fewer opportunities to apply or observe diagnostic or therapeutic frameworks in action. however, this allowed me to strengthen other skills such as program development, systems-level thinking, and culturally responsive engagement.
recommend_reason: i would recommend the iris clubhouse because it’s the kind of placement that teaches you through real, everyday experiences. it’s not clinical, but it challenges you to think critically about systems, access, and how people really recover when they’re given voice and purpose. the director and staff are passionate and supportive, and the members will teach you more than any textbook. it’s a place where you learn by doing, and where you see the impact of social work in motion.
comments_supervisor: my afi consistently met me where i was at and created a space where i could grow, ask questions, and reflect honestly on my learning. she modeled the nasw code of ethics in both her professional conduct and her everyday interactions with staff, members, and me. her commitment to dignity, integrity, and competence was something i witnessed regularly. supervision was meaningful and thoughtful, offering time to process challenges, explore new ideas, and develop my voice as a future social worker. her support helped shape the kind of practitioner i hope to become.
other_comments: it was priceless.

most_helpful: the most helpful learning experience was emotional regulation while dealing with clients who were in imminent threat for suicide.
least_helpful: the least helpful was their supervision sessions. the sessions were too large and as learners we were not given the type of debrief needed for this work.
recommend_reason: i needed a remote practicum and ctl fit this need. the program does amazing work with a vulnerable population in crisis. it was an eye-opening experience, and i will continue to volunteer on the platform.
comments_supervisor: jess was a joy to work with. she is organized and great at her job.
other_comments: i would recommend that future students placed here have their supervision with the university.

agency_name: united way of the lewis and clark area
agency_name_display: United Way Of The Lewis And Clark Area
most_helpful: the most helpful or educational part during my practicum at united way of lewis and clark was gaining hands-on experience with our community and our hmis systems responses to the unsheltered. observing case board meetings and working with the hmis system allowed me to see how agencies coordinate services and use data to identify trends and gaps. this experience deepened my understanding of how strategic planning, resource sharing, and compassion come together to create sustainable solutions for the unsheltered in our comminity.
least_helpful: the one thing that i found was teh least helpful during my practicum was the limited opportunity for direct client interaction.  i did gain alot of valuable experience in with the hmis systems and with work in our community, i found it challenging to connect theory to practice without consistent one-on-one engagement. i would have to say that it made it harder to apply certain clinical skills, which are also important to my development as a social worker.
recommend_reason: i would definitelyin courage another student with similar learning and career goals, especially if they are interested in macro social work, hmis system interventions, and community work with other organizations. i feeel that united way of lewis and clark offers a valuable opportunity to understand how local organizations work together to address homelessness and other community issues.
comments_supervisor: my afi (jeff busher) has been incredibly throughout my entire practicum experience. he provided consistent supervision, encouraged reflection, and created a safe space to ask questions and explore areas of growth. i really enjoyed jeff's willingness to challenge me while also validating my experiences, which helped me build confidence and deepen my understanding of the field.

most_helpful: the opportunity to offer individual counseling and facilitate group and discuss these experiences in supervision.
least_helpful: the least educational aspect of the practicum was the amount of time spent navigating outdated technology. i think it would enhance client care coordination and student education to have a ehr system for client records.
recommend_reason: really it depends on the student. i think students need to have at least two years of direct service work under their belt before jumping into this position. you get a ton of experience in this role and you really get to figure out your style but you need to be a really strong advocate for your needs within this role. my advice to the program is that they make it a required component for this role to record sessions and have the outside msw supervisor watch them with the student.
comments_supervisor: i think the ywca needs to strongly consider hiring on another employed clinician. the clinical department is only employed by one part time clinician who is employed as a manager. carol oversees 5-8 interns at a time. having another clinician there would help tremendously with supervision. there is a lot to cover and do within supervision and we spent a lot of time discussing busy work jobs, logistics, and learning time consuming and confusing systems-many outdated.  carol is not just our clinical supervisor who supports us with our case loads and learning about facilitating groups, she is also our manager. it would support all the interns and the clinical manager to have another employed clinician available for supervision.

most_helpful: the opportunity to work one on one with clients in a therapeutic capacity, and to exercise autonomy and independence with regard to theoretical approaches , styles and dinterventions was very valuable. likewise, completing assessments, progress notes and other tracking and billing requirements was critical. the ywca offers an dintegrated environment for social work students to be exposed to multiple levels of need and support systems, such as housing, dv advocacy and legal support.
least_helpful: there were a few gaps in terms of my clinical experience that my supervisor was not able to fill, such as supporting the creation of treatment plans and practicing diagnostics. there seems to be an expectation the practicum students have already learned certain clinical skills , including family and couples counseling as well as treatment planning and diagnostics, that have not been offered in my program. i imagine this is due to the supervisor being an lcpc and the majority of students being from the counseling/mental health program. additionally, there is a significant level of interaction with cfs and dv law, which also has not been covererd in my msw program and which the supervisor does not have the time or capability to train us in.
recommend_reason: carol is a wonderful supervisor, the experience is rich, and the ywca is a positive, team based environment

most_helpful: the supervision i received and opportunities to learn ethical and trauma informed principles have helped me tremendously this year. i've learned so much about working with couples and actually like it!
least_helpful: i hate that i have to miss attending most provider meetings live but i have enjoyed watching the recordings and following up on any needed clarification.
recommend_reason: this is a phenomenal place to learn especially within the intersection of religion, sexuality, and mental health.
comments_supervisor: natasha is such a great supervisor and i have learned so much from her.
other_comments: this has been a huge growth opportunity for me and i can't wait to continue my career with symmetry counseling.

most_helpful: the team was very welcoming and open to being flexible based on what i wanted to learn and experience i wanted to gain during my practicum. it was a great experience to learn from a variety of clinicians and get opportunity to shadow in other parts of the hospital as well.
least_helpful: nothing!
recommend_reason: great learning opportunity and team.
other_comments: loved doing my practicum here!

most_helpful: the use of supervision at my practicum agency was the most helpful and educational! we had a group supervision once weekly as well as a 1:1 with our supervisor once weekly.
least_helpful: the only thing i can think of is the chaos of the ywca's first step calls. as an advocate, we are responsible for four first step on-call shifts a month and the organization on the y's part was sometimes lacking.
recommend_reason: i think sarc is a great practicum to develop and strength skills!
comments_supervisor: they really made my practicum experience!
other_comments: na

most_helpful: there were numerous aspects of my practicum experience which were helpful. the practicum allowed me to learn within a multidisciplinary team environment in which i was able to observe and work with professionals with a wide variety of experiences and backgrounds. during the practicum, i was given numerous daily opportunities to work with individuals and to facilitate process groups. my afi made himself consistently available to talk both about specific patient situations and overall social work practice. i was treated with respect and trust and was allowed to learn hands-on in a unique and supportive acute in-patient care environment.
least_helpful: i can't think of things that were not helpful about the practicum experience. the environment of acute in-patient care was new for me and was sometimes stressful. i also heard periodically from both patients and staff that there were inconsistencies in care approaches from different staff members, and while i think this is a given in any work environment, there sometimes seemed to be a lack of connection among different roles. however, i was not privy to any situation in which different approaches to care among staff led to diminished outcomes for patients,
recommend_reason: i feel that this was an excellent opportunity to learn about social work practice within a clinical environment. i learned how to use assessment tools, was able to practice chart notes, and was able to talk with therapists and doctors about, and to learn about, clinical diagnoses. i also have a better understanding of community resources and the ways logan health behavioral health engages with community partners. aaron boysen is an outstanding teacher who offers support and the freedom to learn in equal measure,
comments_supervisor: my afi, aaron boysen, was very supportive, provided daily opportunities for discussion and reflection, and is a fantastic teacher.
other_comments: i appreciate the opportunity to do my specialty year practicum at logan health behavioral health. i learned more than i had even expected and have gained needed experience and confidence as i prepare to venture out into the world in this field.

most_helpful: they offer continuing education and catered to the na population in the helena community.
least_helpful: nothing, everything is valuable.
recommend_reason: they provide cultural education and work in all levels of practice.
comments_supervisor: this is a new bh director. we are going through a lot of policy changes. she services on the board of behavioral health. the adjustment period is still under development.
other_comments: change is inevitable and growth producing.

agency_name: center for children, families and workforce development
agency_name_display: Center For Children, Families And Workforce Development
most_helpful: this practicum uses an ongoing iterative process to develop workforce trainings. i was able to gain an understanding of what happens at the macro level of policies and trainings as well as become more comfortable with giving and accepting feedback from my afi and the rest of the team.
least_helpful: n/a. i believe i've learned a lot through this experience.
recommend_reason: i have taken similar trainings at the micro level of practice and had always been curious how they were developed. having worked in direct client care and now learning social work policy from the macro level helped give me a fuller understanding of the scope of social work practice.
comments_supervisor: i plan to pursue my lcsw in the future. my afi never did this, but as i explored the process my afi was very helpful in answering questions and finding resources to look into further. as an adjunct professor my afi also helped me navigate some of the difficulties that came up with switching over to canvas from moodle.

most_helpful: this session paired very well with identifying personal and professional goals and assessing my readiness for leadership. i set out to learn more about non profit administration and i feel more familiar than when i began this half of my practicum.
least_helpful: there was a lot of chaos and not much direction throughout this session. i had to work hard to identify tasks that would meet the expectations of practicum. my afi and the organization had made some commitments that fell through and it created some difficulty in completing many of the tasks i outlined in my learning agreement.
recommend_reason: i think despite the issues i experienced the organization as a whole works very hard to be responsive to feedback, has a strong commitment to their mission and do well to audit their performance and outcomes. i think it is a good environment to learn about non profits.
comments_supervisor: i think that some of the limitations of my roll and my afi's position created some difficulty. i think shelli is amazing but i am not sure she plans to take anymore students.

most_helpful: understanding systems that effect children in crisis. i have learned now to navigate cfs, medicaid, a variety of therapists, parent visitation, head start/mcps/school, etc effectively. also, practical informed learning of how to be a supportive adult to children in crisis who may be dysregulated and traumatized.
least_helpful: probably transporting children to various appointments. it's important to build relationships with providers and school staff for wrap around care. however, driving to the same daycares everyday was not fulfilling. this was an employment based practicum with transport being party of my job so i'm certainly not complaining.
recommend_reason: experiential learning, opportunity to directly practice with children, and management is extraordinarily supportive and are genuinely invested in a student's education. vast opportunity to apply theory, research, and advancing human rights.
comments_supervisor: based on barb's mentorship, i could write a 10 page report in apa. barb is the kind of adviser who changes your life, this is certainly true for me. she guided me to use various lenses and perspectives when working with families. through her wisdom and experience i have gained essential and practical skills to confidently navigate and guide my practice. most of all, barb leads by example and has an authentic interest in contributing to the positive advancement of social workers. i can't say enough about how barb has helped me become more compassionate and sharpen my professional skills, what an honor it is to watch her in action and benefit from her awesomeness.

most_helpful: unfortunately, the budget plan that i had created for riverwalk was never allowed to be reviewed by supervisors because the closing announcement happened before there was an opportunity to share potential alternartives. however, the research and creation of the plan was very helpful. shadowing, creating, and co-facilitating groups at the recovery center has been educational and fulfilling in ways that go beyond words.
least_helpful: typical challenges that are associated with our current behavioral health system tied to limited funding.

most_helpful: weekly supervision with my afi, the ability to shadow other clinicians, and meeting with clients. meeting with clients was easily the most helpful because it really challenged me in applying knowledge gained from the program while also looking towards additional evidence-informed practices that support the needs of each individual i meet with.
least_helpful: i cannot think of anything that was the least helpful or educational which i am grateful for.
recommend_reason: the staff is incredibly supportive and is always willing to answer any questions i have. i never felt that i was left alone on an issue. i have experience as a certified behavior technician and i felt that i was pushed into the field without the proper preparation. my experience at bhr could not be further from that. i went through onboarding, had discussions with my afi where she provided a multitude of resources, and had opportunities to shadow other clinicians, allowing me to settle a bit into the position before beginning to work with clients. even when i did begin to work with clients, my caseload gradually increased which was very helpful.
comments_supervisor: katherine has an open door policy to where i could come to her to ask questions outside of our scheduled weekly supervision. she is supportive and always provides any resources that may be beneficial for me and my clients. she also challenges me. when i come to ask questions, she asks what my thoughts are before providing her own suggestions. her feedback has been helpful as well.

most_helpful: weekly supervision with stacy, as well as the social emotional groups i was able to lead. i also received management skills through my promotion to program manager.
recommend_reason: friends is very strength-based and provides a well rounded learning environment that includes 1:1 support and advocacy for clients, incredible supervision, and a wonderful team culture.
comments_supervisor: stacy is amazing and i am thankful each day to have such a wonderful mentor! her support during supervision has been the main reason i continue to grow as a social worker!

most_helpful: weekly supervision.
least_helpful: na
recommend_reason: helpful

most_helpful: working 1 on 1 with clients.
least_helpful: support for students juggling multiple professional roles. i found that while my instructor was wonderful in helping out, certain deadlines and a tight timelines made it difficult to manage both my full-time job, practicum role and class workload in a way that allowed me to fully engage with my practicum role in the way that i would have liked to. it would be beneficial to allow more wiggle room in terms of final practicum dates for the program moving forward.
recommend_reason: great support from staff with both crisis text line and my instructor.

most_helpful: working directly with patients during meal support, therapeutic groups, mindfulness sessions, and moments of dysregulation has been especially impactful in helping me learn how to offer interventions in real time. these experiences have strengthened my confidence in setting boundaries within group settings and in facilitating arts-based, music, and yoga nidra groups. i've also gained valuable insights from my coworkers — not just about therapeutic theories and clinical skills, but how to thoughtfully apply them in practice. their guidance has played a key role in my growth and development as a future clinician.
least_helpful: some weeks, it felt like i was constantly in meetings: team meetings, individual supervision, group supervision, and even outside supervision. while i deeply value the support and guidance from my supervisors and team, there were times when the volume of these meetings left me feeling emotionally drained and mentally burnt out. i found myself wishing i could fast-forward to the point where i’ve completed my degree and can step fully into the role of a full-time clinician. i am feeling the demands of being a practicum student and online student for three years.
recommend_reason: i genuinely believe in the holistic approach of our treatment program. throughout my time here, i’ve learned so much about eating disorders, personality disorders, and the complex behaviors that often accompany them. i’ve especially appreciated working within a multidisciplinary team and learning from the strong, compassionate leadership that guides our work.

most_helpful: working with a dynamic, interdisciplinary team. ease of access to supervision
least_helpful: i have worked at thrive for several years, so there are areas where i could have been challenged further had i selected a new practicum site.
recommend_reason: thrive is a fun, meaningful place to work. the leaders care deeply about their team members and their success. i felt like a valuable asset to the team and was able to learn a diverse range of skills.
comments_supervisor: betsy is an incredible supervisor who challenged me and my ways of knowing. she cares about me as a person and as a growing social worker.
other_comments: thrive was an excellent placement for both years of learning as there was ample opportunity to grow and develop my social work skills

most_helpful: the most helpful was the expose to the focus areas that i had regarding dv, sa, stalking, human trafficking, dating violence, mmip
least_helpful: having to read from a law dictionary and learn about a bunch of different material within a short amount of time. i feel like i barely scratched the surface of dv/ sa/dating violence/stalking/human trafficking because i was too busy reading about the reauthorization of the vawa act or federal housing programs, and then having to cypher out the different terms and definitions relating to the act itself or different programs. if someone is up for it i would say go for it, but if you need money or do not have the means to be able to drive to downtown billings everyday, then i would say better not try and find something else.
recommend_reason: this agency needs someone who is really focused and understanding of violent crime issues relating to indian country, and the individual would need to have a lot of patience because it is a lot of repetition relating to subject matter, and dedication because of the amount of legwork and communication one has to do to get business done. the organization is a non-profit and hardly has money to pay the staff currently working, so it would be extremely difficult for someone to work there if they need a paying job, or would need some kind of incentive to work there. it is a busy workplace day to day, and the location of the office is kinda scary as well. the mnwc doesn't pay for a parking pass so you have to walk like 2 blocks to get to the office, or pay like 20- 50 dollars a month for parking from the city. homeless run around in the streets, and it is like scary during the winter time because of the darkness setting in early.
comments_supervisor: it was ok during the first semester, but the second semester was too much because i was overloaded and did not and was not given adequate time, instruction, etc because of the business of events the organization had during the month of march/april. i would try to get the learning agreement assignments completed, but i would always have a "project" placed in front of me and that would need to be completed first before my la assignments because of the nearing dates of calander events the organization was hosting. i felt pushed to the side and then had to rush towards the end to complete my assignments.
other_comments: i think that i did learn alot maybe more so within policy work and advocacy than most of my cohort. i was able to receive great instruction regarding grant management, reporting, and other areas relating to organization function and management. i really put everything that i had into wanting to learn about how organizations function and how policy work and advocacy help the local community here in billings. i appreciate the instruction that i received, but it was extremely difficult and at times downright awful because of the financial implications that amounted over time.  i had migraines every week ,and i had to work a second job during the evenings/nights so i could pay the bills and survive.

agency_name_display: Center For Children, Families And Workforce Development
most_helpful: the ability to reach out to anyone in the office for ideas or help on different aspects of a project. getting to know the other students and who could be relied on for some guidance or editing or creative ideas. the center does a 'show and share' once a month, where they bring in community professionals and i always learned a lot from those opportunities.
least_helpful: minimal guidance in a position i had never done before was difficult. minimal guidance and sometimes a lack of projects or feedback on how to move forward on projects. supervision being cancelled without reason and feeling like my afi did not know what my project was or how to guide me.
recommend_reason: this is a tough question, it seems like many students struggle with the lack of guidance as well as feeling like the work we do is not taken into account. it also, often feels like projects are without purpose, or if there is purpose we are not privy to the information. i did not feel valued and often felt like the work i was doing was a waste of time. perhaps it would be different during a legislative year, when expectations are maybe more clear. expectations usually felt fuzzy around the edges and i felt expected to just know what was wanted.

most_helpful: the most educational portion of my practicum agency was the multitude of learning opportunities that were at our hands. as far as a msw student goes it was difficult prior to working with um to find a path that was aligned best with msw learning agreement needs. i think it taught me to use my voice and trust my experience which is very valuable.
least_helpful: the team at my practicum agency was often disjointed which impacted the ability to communicate within this small agency. when i would ask questions i would be redirected around the office to various people. i often had to find answers myself which is definitely a great learning opportunity to a point. i found this to hinder learning opportunities as well because the answers at times were not there. i wish i had been told the newness of this agency because i think it really speaks to the culture within the office and the disjointedness. finding a specific activity for a msw student proved to be difficult and confusing for myself and the office as a whole.
recommend_reason: i would recommend it with stipulations. i would go in to interview and be very specific with what they wanted to do and ask if this was possible (of course if this was a need) within the agency. this placement is great to learn about the community and social work agencies but more so at an undergraduate level as opposed to a graduate level. if it is at a graduate level i think the student, um, and the irc have to work harder to ensure the student gets what they need from this placement. this need at times, i found, was difficult to meet for the irc. i was lucky to come in at a time wherein a group needed to be formed based on partnership with the school of social work at um which i had time to implement. this placement will not work at this time for clinical focus, this works for mezzo and some micro practice focus, but moreover this is a basic learning from social work agencies and nuances.
comments_supervisor: i initially started by having jen barile as my afi which then shifted at the second semester to jesse littman. jen barile struggled to have time to meet with us and when we met and i brought things up they were often met with a sense of lack of caring, perhaps apathy, and it was very discouraging. she is very down to earth but this seems to impact the possibility portion of a new practicum student, it was very harbouring to think about what could be. in working with jen barile she would often just direct me to someone else with my question. next, working with jesse littman she was able to make time to meet weekly to talk through various learning opportunities. the first semester working with jen and jesse was very confusing but the second semester was much easier working with jesse. finally, i don't think the work i emailed to the team or my afi often actually got looked at, so, when i would ask for feedback it was as if no one actually read what i sent. it seems irc has many time restrictions and needs students to help free up this time but cannot give much to the students. that being said it is what you make it and i think there is a lot of growth the irc can do in the community and a msw student who is determined enough can do so and make it into a great learning opportunity like i feel ultimately i was able to do thanks to all the guidance and help from my seminar class, and msw classes.
other_comments: i think this opportunity is a great one but i think it is worth noting what i've already highlighted and again that this is not a clinical focus and has very little (if any ability to be so), and the irc has limited time, resources, and effort to put into a student. so, while i think they do really need students i think this is worth noting for the student moving into this position so they can advocate for themselves. i think without kat's help and advocacy efforts i would have struggled a lot more at this placement for the aforementioned reasons, but, because of her help i was able to use her wisdom to see the goals at the end and the real world learning opportunities these provided.

most_helpful: i loved the inclusion into the team during my second semester.  i was paired with an individual who also has a bsw and that helped tremendously to see families through the same lens of human dignity and respect.  i was given tasks and responsibilities almost immediately, which helped guide me in the learning process and helped to hone existing skills.  i also appreciated how involved and supportive the supervisor was.
least_helpful: during my 1st semester, i was paired with an individual who did not have a social work background.  this was difficult for me at first because i don't see the families we serve through a punitive lens.  this person also was not a good fit for my hands on learning style as she was insistent that i simply observe and not ask questions during our investigations.  i was able to work with collateral support and help build a better picture of the family.  building those relationships within the community will be helpful in the future.  toward the end of the semester i chose to look at this mismatch of personality types as a blessing and to try and view the world through her lens.  i wish i would have been able to spend time with multiple workers during the investigation portion of the practicum.
recommend_reason: this field is very intense and very hard.  spending 9 month within the department, you will see many aspects of this job.  it will take you places you never knew existed.  i think it gives a good overview and will help individuals determine if this is what this is really what they want to do rather than taking a job and then becoming overwhelmed.
comments_supervisor: i was very satisfied with my supervision during second semester and not very satisfied with supervision during my first semester.  there were two different supervisors and two different supervision styles.
other_comments: i appreciate the opportunities and experiences during this last year of my educational journey.  this has been an amazing time, covid-19 and all. :-)

most_helpful: the variety of social work opportunities.  i got to experience aspects of clinical and macro level social work.  there was opportunities to shape my practicum to meet my needs.

recommend_reason: while the work can be hard and it can be slow to to start especially if you have not had any experience in the field you have the ability to speak your needs as a practicum student and be able to shape your experience.  an example is starting out work at the group home when they have no experience working with individuals given an sdmi diagnosis may not sound fun however, once they learn a little about the clientele they can then take part in writing continued stay reviews which is an assessment of group homes clients progress and assessment for current need for group home setting.  or for me i was able to be given a great opportunity work with bham and be on a comity to address mental health case management reimbursement cuts and come you with a plan to help increase reimbursement while also coming up with a way to meet needs to track success.
comments_supervisor: my afi was amazing. as a graduate of the msw program here she had a sense of pride in wanting to make the practicum experience the best it experience possible.  not only would she try to shape our practicum in ways that would fit us but would also challenge us to think about social work ethics, our own basis, and even social work practices.

least_helpful: the shoestring budget and financial pressures from the capitalist system that requires employees to manage unhealthy caseloads beyond capacity make for a challenging learning environment.
recommend_reason: yes, provided the student understands how to implement strong boundaries to preserve their own mental health and can navigate hierarchical structures with strong self-advocacy skills. darren ashby lcsw lac is more than a competent, trauma-informed mentor and patient teacher.
comments_supervisor: supervision was incredibly helpful and useful for long-term practice goals and applicable to more than just the individual case discussed. darren helped me look at the issue, conflict, and solution-focused resources as both short-term and long-term solutions and how these problems/solutions can be generalized.
other_comments: the reality of working/learning within a capitalist system interferes with a practicum student's ability to engage in policy or executive learning. i believe the university could benefit students by requiring work/learning hours to be outlined in writing in a contract. this would help alleviate the power imbalance that students are placed in within these systems of care.

recommend_reason: i honestly don't know if i would recommend this practicum site to another student, mainly because my first-year supervisor has been very difficult to work with. my experience with casey, my current afi was very different. the organization as a whole is pretty amazing. if some of the hr issues were dealt with i would absolutely encourage other students to complete their practicum here. i know that some of these difficulties are being addressed, i'm just not sure where it will go from here.
comments_supervisor: i have felt supported by casey. i was able to make the best use of our supervision. by creating an agenda that guided us from week to week. he had lots of feedback as well which was always appreciated.

most_helpful: i appreciated the immersive, hands-on approach that my practicum provided.  the opportunity to work with seasoned mental health therapists who are deeply committed to their profession allowed me to learn best practices simply by being a witness to the work.  the structured supervision and feedback sessions were also invaluable, which not only allowed me to learn skills, but increased my understanding of ethical-decision making alongside client-centered care.
least_helpful: one area of my practicum that could be enhanced is the allocation of roles. as a licensed addiction counselor, my exposure to mental health assessments was limited, primarily learning through case staffing since i worked with sud clients, which the agency was able to bill for. allocating some of my available time to shadow mental health therapists may have provided more in-depth learning, allowing me to understand how to integrate addiction counseling with mental health strategies. a change to this would enrich the practicum experience by offering a more rounded education.
recommend_reason: my final practicum placement at gvhc is an integrated care clinic that offers many opportunities to students whose learning and career goals are similar to my own.  gvhc provides many opportunities for professional growth due to it's commitment of integrated care, ethical practices, and many avenues of healthcare.  the staff at gvhc offer a wide range of experiences to learn from, while also being supportive of students working to learn and enhance understanding.  it's important to note that self-advocacy is an important part of being successful at this placement, as without it, there will be few opportunities offered.  a student must be willing to pursue educational opportunities as they arise, and not expect others at the agency to  "assign" them.  i have witnessed students do both, and those that are able to advocate for learning opportunities are those that succeed.
comments_supervisor: mr. mccracken offered awesome guidance to me throughout my second semester of the practicum.  weekly supervision was not only used to discuss client cases, but also aided me in ethical-decision making models and engaging debates that typically centered around topics directly related to my practicum class, sw488.  this supervision improved the quality and efficacy of my practicum overall.
other_comments: thank you for this opportunity!

most_helpful: i have had a unique opportunity to grow my program/agency skills, which includes networking, presentation giving, and referral opportunities for clients.  i really enjoyed being given a platform to build a sud program that embodies social work ethics and strength-based care.  my afi is very knowledgeable about the um program, and was able to connect me with concepts and theories that wil be useful for years to come.
least_helpful: the constraints of a bsw lead to less client social work contact due to medicaid not offering reimbursement for student contact, but my lac license gave me opportunities to work with clients within the confines of sud diagnoses. this led to generalized sw practice via advocating, connecting clients to resources, and engaging in the planned change process.  there was little opportunity for anti-racism practice, as all of the clients i met with at gvhc were caucasian, but i was able to fulfill this learning activity to a much higher degree when working at recovery centers of montana during this year's first semester.
recommend_reason: if the person has a license, i think it would be good for them to understand that an agency will want time with clients to be paid time, reimbursable by medicaid or other insurance agencies.  this agency would be a good placement for someone interested in school therapy though, as there are many school based therapists to shadow with some willingness to advocate for yourself.
comments_supervisor: i very much appreciated tom's previous experience with cynthia garthwait as an instructor, and enjoyed his understanding of her teachings.  i often brought topics that were taught in sw488 to my supervision, and he was always able to dive into the subject in a way that i hadn't considered, and seemed to have a knack to bring forward ethical concerns from the teachings.  he is also very helpful with documentation and clinical questions, which is not taught often in the school setting.
other_comments: i believe a policy change at the state level would give more practicum students the ability to provide practicum agencies utilization of the student contribution.  if there is room for this going forward that i can be a part of, please let me know!

recommend_reason: absolutely. it's been a great experience. anyone who is wanting to be involved in the legal system or who wants to help make changes/feel like they are making a change i highly recommend they get involved here at the muni court.

most_helpful: i had the opportunity every day to be in front of clients.  the other social workers welcomed me as part of the team.  i received support when i needed it, it has been a great experience.
least_helpful: nothing.  it has been a huge learning opportunity.
recommend_reason: there is so much work to be done, and so many students need emotional support, it is a busy, challenging, and never boring practicum.  it can be overwhelming, but that makes us grow.
comments_supervisor: i have my afi that i meet with weekly, but there is an msw here at the site with me.  i can call her or meet with her anytime.  she has been wonderful.
other_comments: what a huge learning experience.  so much client time.  i am feeling more comfortable and i am excited to see what next year holds.

most_helpful: kayli was an amazing afi. she was able to help support my growth when i was unsure of my progress and helped me discover where i needed to improve and where i was already succeeding. i really enjoyed my small cohort model of being with other msw students working together but also separately with different focuses within the um community.
least_helpful: the most difficult part for me was the on boarding process. the well-being support coordinators were run this year as a pilot program without the necessary lead time to make sure policies and processes were well established an in place. many of these policies or procedures have fallen into place over the last year. for someone with a chaotic life outside of school, structure would have been helpful here.
recommend_reason: the wellness department/center has one of the best afi options available. i heard many msw students describe supervisors that either didn't have enough time to dedicate to them or their learning. i really appreciated kayli's willingness to step in when i needed help but to also allow me chances to test my growth within appropriate settings. kayli's optimistic outlook and kindness was instrumental in my learning and how i would like to approach my future work.
comments_supervisor: my afi was kayli julius and she was amazing. my supervision was provided by amy capulupo. i was not entirely sure about my experiences with amy inside of my first semester. i thought that she was probably doing exactly what should be done but still was unsure. after taking dynamic supervision, amy very clearly is invested in building trust and helping to work through issues that her supervisees might be going through. i appreciated amy's ability to be frank about current events and their impact on us as msw students but also as humans. she worked with me when i had personal issues arise and difficulty this year. i felt really supported in my learning and growth potential.

most_helpful: kayli was extremely amazing and supportive throughout the entire year. she was so helpful every single step of the way and really provided a strong foundation for my learning. amy also provided such incredible supervision and was equally amazing in creating an environment that really made me feel comfortable bringing questions about clients into the space or talking about my own personal struggles and asking for support.
least_helpful: bureaucratic things out of my control.
recommend_reason: i learned a ton in helping to develop a new program, working alongside kayli and my fellow wbsc’s, and working with a demographic that i  don’t have a lot of experience working with.
comments_supervisor: i really can’t say enough good things about kayli. she was always available for questions or feedback. she is easily one of the most supportive supervisors i’ve ever had. and i appreciated all of her feedback and support.
other_comments: it’s been so much better than i expected.

most_helpful: supportive supervisor, hands on learning
least_helpful: not being directly on site
recommend_reason: it's a good placement if you're interested in working with kids.
comments_supervisor: excellent supervisor, supportive, helpful

most_helpful: the flexibility to structure my own workload and projects.
least_helpful: sometimes that flexibility left me feeling unsupported and unsure.
recommend_reason: this practicum agency allows for all levels of social work practice, micro, mezzo, and macros, depending on what you want your focus to be, there is flexibility to really tailor your practicum experience to what you want it to be. like i mentioned, this was awesome, but it took me a while to get used to and took me a while to find the direction i wanted to go in, as my experience was totally up to me.
comments_supervisor: my afi and i were a good fit for communication needs and styles.

most_helpful: the most helpful part of my practicum agency was learning about how change comes about (or doesn't) in a government agency. having time away from my normal job to dedicate to learning and diving deeper into social work was very valuable.
least_helpful: i would just recommend having an afi or at least working with other social workers who understand social work as a profession and practice. my afi has a masters in public administration and had no idea what social work was about.
recommend_reason: in an ideal world, i would say no. i don't think that this agency has the framework or mindset to support social work learning. its a government agency in a rural county, with a ton of built in racism, bigotry, and very very slow moving change. i am very invested in being a part of that change so it was valuable to me (and i got to do an ebp) but for someone else, i would not recommend it.
comments_supervisor: i really appreciate the time that my afi took to train me and help me critically think. he helped my professional development greatly.

most_helpful: the most helpful/educational part of my practicum agency was supervision with my afi. i was able to take a real world situation, speak with my afi about it, have my afi observe my handling of the situation, then go back and reflect on my performance with my afi.
least_helpful: i don't know that i can really say that anything was least helpful, but i can say that the agency's administration and company culture caused a lot of challenges. that being said, this presented many opportunities to practice navigating choppy waters, so to speak.
recommend_reason: i wouldn't be able to recommend another practicum student to any other program than csct, as i don't know how the other programs work. csct, however, has many passionate and ethical employees who are more than willing to share their wisdom. additionally, csct is an opportunity for a student to work in multiple lanes of social work, as csct employees work with families, children, in the school, and in the community.
comments_supervisor: jen certa is a phenomenal afi and supervision was the highlight of my practicum.

most_helpful: the one on one sessions doing counseling practice with individual students, as well as the weekly debriefs with my afi and the counseling team.
least_helpful: i do wish that my practicum agency had someone on staff who had their msw for 2 years. while connecting with other students in the program was nice, they all had very different experiences and it would have been helpful to get that shared experience with other people with experience working in schools.
recommend_reason: i loved my experience, however, i do not think i would have been successful as an intern had it not been an employment based practicum. there was so much about the school, its culture, and its innerworkings that my afi did not have to teach me. i do not think it would be possible to work full time and do a practicum here without your full time job also being at the school.
comments_supervisor: she is amazing! our weekly check ins made the experience so enjoyable and i felt like she really cared about my learning experience.

most_helpful: the variety of backgrounds that i was able to interact with and having the freedom to be creative with clients about how to overcome their challenges.
recommend_reason: my practicum is committed to helping me succeed in my educational and professional goals and they allow me the freedom to interact with clients how we seem helpful.

most_helpful: i had an opportunity to engage in the competencies in many ways, so there was always a lot to learn and in many different applications. there are also a lot of resources available for my learning with my agency, and if my colleagues or supervisor weren't able to support me, there are so many other knowledgeable staff at the agency. it's also been very helpful that the agency has been so accommodating and flexible with my schedule and upholding work-life balance.
least_helpful: there were times where i was quite overwhelmed with workload, but not reaching out about this (i did reach out once) was my choice, and so next time i will be more proactive here.
recommend_reason: supportive staff, great benefits and work-life balance (it's not perfect, but great compared to other social service agencies), you really feel you're making a positive impact in people lives, everyday.

most_helpful: i liked that i had the opportunity to practice telehealth case management work towards the end of the spring semester. i also enjoyed being involved with our family based services (fbs) program to see how to provide more support and resources to caregivers and families of the youth that our agency serves. i also enjoyed attending conferences, such as the pediatric symposium this spring and the community of practice conference last fall. while youth dynamics might be considered more of a micro practice setting, i enjoyed the outreach opportunities to engage with various coalitions, such as community management teams and the impact coalition. these gave me the more insight into how various social service agencies in the community can work together to help area families.
least_helpful: while youth dynamics was an employment-based practicum, i was still a newer employee when i started the msw program, so a lot of what occurred during my practicum placement was also job training and very educational. i also had the mutual benefit of being able to use knowledge gained from various classes to apply towards agency practices during my placement.
recommend_reason: for someone wanting to work in youth mental health, i think youth dynamics is a good placement to get the scope of what mental health care looks like for children in montana. we provide wrap-around services to youth and families, so you can make connections with various agencies through this work, while also gaining a real-world understanding of the challenges youth mental health faces in a rural state. pending on the area, there are a wide range of what a practicum placement can look like, such as working as a case or care manager, in a youth home setting, or day treatment center.
comments_supervisor: while regular meetings were difficult some weeks, mischelle was always available to answer any questions and provide feedback on client cases, agency policies, and how what i was learning in class can be applied within the agency. she is a wealth of information on youth dynamics policy and procedure and made sure i always did my due diligence regarding client care.
other_comments: even when my first practicum placement didn't work out, the field team and my afi did great work to make sure i didn't fall behind in my hours or learning.

most_helpful: i really enjoyed the hands on experience and being able to see what a social worker can do in a school setting.
least_helpful: i do not have anything that was least helpful.
recommend_reason: social workers are important in schools and i hope schools continue to grow in providing space for them.

comments_supervisor: i think kelly was extremely helpful this year and provided great guidance.
other_comments: nope

other_comments: i really appreciate the opportunity to complete an employment-based practicum.

agency_name: germane and wise mental health consulting
agency_name_display: Germane And Wise Mental Health Consulting
most_helpful: my afi had me research interventions to help with dmdd and ptsd for clients struggling with that. i found that useful because i was able to put together a document on what it looks like and how we can help others. i also found it helpful that i was able to get two perspectives when discussing with my afi and then discussing cases with my msw supervisor. it helped add more tools to my toolbelt i think. additionally, i really liked how we are essentially doing medical social work, so it expands my knowledge base and skillset. we also do a lot of assessment and intervention which is extremely beneficial.
least_helpful: least helpful or educational was probably the high caseload. i have been used to having a high caseload with this job so i am used to it, but i feel social work is more personable and having over 100 patient cases felt more distant. conducting most of our interactions over the phone also feels less personable as well.
recommend_reason: as i mentioned above, we are essentially doing medical social work. we learn how to talk to people, discuss treatment plans, build rapport, advocate for them with doctors, and be their go to person in relation to psychiatric needs. it is experience that i have found to be extremely helpful. once done, you have a wide range of skills as well as knowledge of different psychiatric medications and how they work. i think overall the agency is great.
comments_supervisor: i also found it helpful to have the outside msw supervisor. she was great. both her supervision and my afi together really helped my knowledge base grow.
other_comments: i think germane and wise offers really great experience and i am grateful i was able to do an employment based practicum for my generalist year.

most_helpful: my supervision with elein has been transformative. before this practicum i had never worked in the field and much of this was new for me. i learned more than i ever thought possible. meeting with elein weekly and also knowing that she was always availble if i needed her provided a great deal of peace and support throught this year. elein gave me direction, instruction, guidance, and the confidence to jump in and have an incredibly successful practicum. in addition elein gave me her trust and autonomy to work through problems and laugh with me when i messed up. i honestly can't believe that in a few weeks i won't be at the refuge but the relationships i've built with the clinical staff and other team members will be part of my network of resources for my next practicum and my career.
least_helpful: nothing. i was able to have a client load, co-faciliate three groups, and also get to know the departments and services available at the refuge as well as other resources in the community.
recommend_reason: my experience could not have been better and the support from elein and the rest of the clinical team has been warm, welcoming, and so much more than i could have expected.
comments_supervisor: this is a incredible opportunity for students want to learn, grow, and develop while literally doing the work!
other_comments: another huge strength that my practicum offered was the opportunity to learn from so many incredible clincians and to be supported, listened to, and devleoped by them in their own unique ways will be something i will always remember.

recommend_reason: this particular configuration allows for creativity and responsiveness in a unique way.
other_comments: i suggest the field education team revisit the learning agreement form and include more from cswe around indigenous approach to social work. also, cswe seems more dialed into diversity and anti-racist/anti-bias practices than nasw. let's go deeper in this direction!

agency_name: center for children, families and workforce development
