most_helpful: i have learned a great deal about crisis intervention skills, community partner collaboration, legislation pertaining to sa, and the legal system as a whole throughout my time at star. observing and partaking within the sart process, legal advocacy work through court accompaniment, and working the 24 hour crisis line were all learning opportunities that i found educational.
least_helpful: i honestly didn't find anything that was not helpful or educational. there were certainly slow times or moments throughout where i didn't have a specific task to engage in but that allowed me personal time to learn more about other community agencies, referral processes, etc.
recommend_reason: i didn't have any knowledge about the sart process or day to day advocacy work/case management that is required for this type of work prior to star. i have never worked as a case manager which i think is beneficial for every social work student to do in one form or another. this has really allowed me to have a better understanding of case management and crisis intervention.
comments_supervisor: laura hill was amazing to work with.
other_comments: star provided me with an awesome practicum experience.

most_helpful: i loved the education-focused atmosphere at youth homes. the agency provides ample training opportunities and supervisors are constantly working with employees to help them achieve their career goals.
least_helpful: mid-way through my second year practicum my afi left youth homes causing a small break in the supervision i received as i moved to a different afi.
recommend_reason: youth homes is a great organization that really cares about the professional and personal development of its employees. the agency's culture is incredibly supportive, allowing a new practitioner the guidance they need to grow their skills and feel capable of taking on their new roles.
comments_supervisor: when the afi change happened during my practicum, i was reduced to meeting with my afi less frequently. i was nervous about this at first but found that i was still supported through my colleagues at the agency enough to still feel comfortable seeing clients.

most_helpful: i was able to balance working one on one with clients with also engaging in community level work.
least_helpful: i really can't think of anything!
recommend_reason: bridgercare offers so many different opportunities and avenues for learning. whether you're interested in micro level work or macro, bridgercare has some kind of initiative that fits the bill!
comments_supervisor: i wouldn't have had this opportunity without alanna. she truly lifts other people up and supports them.

most_helpful: i was able to utilize social work practices every day.
least_helpful: i can't think of anything that was not helpful in some way at my practicum. it was an amazing place to complete a practicum.
recommend_reason: i would absolutely recommend university of phoenix as a practicum placement to another student. it is such a positive and warm atmosphere that a person immediately feels welcome. they focus on a growth mindset of learning with keeping their employees and students as top priorities at all times.
comments_supervisor: my afi and outside msw supervisor were both absolutely amazing and supportive.
other_comments: it was a great experience where i learned a lot of important skills.

most_helpful: i was pushed to move out of my comfort zone and do things i normally would not do.
least_helpful: the negative people contributing to the school's climate were not helpful.
recommend_reason: it was eye opening. the student behaviors were out of control at times. it was a very good learning experience trying to deal with all of that.

most_helpful: individual supervision sessions where i was able to process through challenging situations or ask sometimes too basic questions with great support (and no judgement on my basic questions) and a capacity to allow me to process through the situation without just giving me answers. the level of support from jeremy was tremendous and really made the experience enjoyable, educational, and challenging.
least_helpful: the school year ramped up quickly with activity along with my limited schedule to participate in the practicum due to my work schedule did not allow me to be able to observe through any exit interviews, intakes, or termination sessions. observing some of the procedures would have allowed me to mentally wrap my brain around the process. but i was supported by my afi, jeremy o'conner, who walked me through each of the procedures along with support from other interns.
recommend_reason: there are many opportunities to gain tangible and meaningful hands-on experience in cys. the support from the staff and the environment is very positive and inclusive. it is a great place to have a first or generalist experience. there is also the capacity for specialization as well.
comments_supervisor: jeremy is a tremendous support and provided opportunity for individual and group supervision. sessions were as frequent as i needed and sometimes more when necessary. jeremy had an open door policy and was always open and willing to talk.also, margie diaz, who is not an afi, but the program director, was also a great support.
other_comments: if there any any umt students in the los angeles/orange county area, california youth services is a tremendous program to work with!

agency_name: department of health and human services
agency_name_display: Department Of Health And Human Services
most_helpful: it was very diverse in terms of clients and experiences. i learned a lot about the work that i am passionate about, and working with different populations has been very eye opening.
least_helpful: because of the nature of the work, there wasn't always opportunities for me to learn more about assessments and evaluations.
recommend_reason: there are many learning opportunities in this role, and if a student would like to learn more about working with people in crisis, this is definitely the place to do.
comments_supervisor: sarah was always asking me what i wanted to get out of my practicum. she provided learning opportunities for me from whatever i asked to learn more about.

most_helpful: kayli was a fountain of knowledge and always willing to support and collaborate. it was a really good opportunity to practice a lot of skills (motivational interviewing, therapeutic silences, case managing, outreach). it provided a variety of opportunities for learning and professional and personal growth.
recommend_reason: i think the variety of learning opportunities is extremely valuable. it's also really flexible with opportunities to make the experience what you want at times. kayli is also just an amazing, understanding, brilliant, supportive, compassionate, and collaborative supervisor...which makes such a difference in really thriving through practicum.

most_helpful: learning about all of the policies in training and the monthly meetings helped me to learn new skills to apply directly to working with children. the most recent meeting entailed how to apply components from the circle of security, which has been found to be very effective — topics such as this relate to my program because they can be connected to therapy skills and self-regulation skills. teaching self-regulation has been one of my main goals. also, working with all different types of children, employees, and supervisors has been very beneficial for my education. speaking to parents who may come from homes with diverse circumstances has will also inform me of how to communicate with all different types of people from various backgrounds in my future practice.
least_helpful: at times, there have been major transitions at bgca, which has caused some confusion at times with policies, scheduling, etc. the turnover of some employees created some turbulence, but overall, did not affect the quality of my practicum, since i am primilary working with children and families directly.
recommend_reason: yes because there has been great support from staff. there are many opportunities to form relationships with people from all different backgrounds. if someone cannot work with high-stress behaviors, this may not be the practicum for them — it requires a lot of patience and reflection on how to improve the quality of care from an informed, ethical, and empathetic approach.
comments_supervisor: kezia and lee have been amazing. lee no longer works there but kezia has always been very supportive, transparent, and provided guidance when needed.
other_comments: na

most_helpful: learning to support people with emotionally charged decisions and topics in a limited amount of time, in a high level professional setting that operates at a fast pace.
least_helpful: it has 100% been helpful!
recommend_reason: everyday, in every interaction i was learning new resources, insurance guidelines, organizational protocol, who to advocate to, etc. you are on your toes in each moment
comments_supervisor: i could not have asked for a better supervisor. she perfectly balanced teaching and encouraging, grace and vulnerability. i will take her words and presence with me for a long time. i hope to reflect her level of affective advocacy, educating, and caring in my own work. christine is an amazing social worker and leader. a gentle powerhouse.
other_comments: i am extremely happy with my experience.

most_helpful: my supervision was the most helpful. being able to meet with my supervisor once a week and then as needed through email was extremely helpful.
least_helpful: there wasn't anything that i felt was least helpful.
recommend_reason: i would absolutely recommend grandview to anyone looking for experience as a clinical social worker. the entire team was so kind and helpful, and they made sure i had everything i needed. i felt supported and knew that help was close if needed. it was a wonderful experience.

most_helpful: on-going consultation about cases with supervisor and colleagues
least_helpful: changing to a new supervisor halfway through
recommend_reason: the counseling department provided me with hands-on experience as a therapist. i would recommend this to another student if this is what they intend to do after they graduate the msw program.

most_helpful: participating in interdisciplinary team meetings (rounds, huddles, etc.).
least_helpful: some of the clerical work, like scheduling appointments for patients.
recommend_reason: while hospital case management is a pretty specific area of social work, i think the skills involved can translate to other areas. i would caution anyone interested in direct services, specifically those that allow for long-term relationship building, that hospital case management doesn't often provide that experience. it's still worthwhile, in my opinion.
comments_supervisor: supervision provided great teaching/learning opportunities for both me and my afi. i can certainly see its value in our profession.

most_helpful: real-world, real-time application of skills. the learning experiences occurred often and were extremely beneficial.
recommend_reason: the social workers in the billings schools base programs possess a wealth of knowledge. there are quite a few that would be wonderful supervisors for future msw students.
comments_supervisor: katy is amazing. i am so blessed to have had this opportunity for my practicum journey. she has graciously offered to continue supervision for my licensure.

most_helpful: the availability for weekly supervision and support from my supervisors and afi. all of which are licensed social workers. my practicum agency provides crisis services for a nine counties. therefore, ample amounts of learning opportunities are available at micro, mezzo, and macro levels.
least_helpful: i found myself learning something in every practicum related task or activity. as a new clinical manager, i hold a great deal of responsibility ensuring clinically appropriate crisis services are provided to nine counties. therefore, i must remain mindful of trauma informed care in every task and duty i perform including interacting with staff, volunteers, and partnering agencies.
recommend_reason: the communities served rely heavily on my practicum agency for crisis and suicide interventions. practicum students are exposed to crisis interventions at all levels of acuity, students are supported by licensed social workers at various career levels. students have access to crisis and suicide intervention training, laws and legislature, and various protocols among various emergency services agencies, law enforcement, and hospitals.
comments_supervisor: supervision was helpful in providing space to self-reflect on the connection between learning goals and tasks performed in a fact-paced crisis center.

most_helpful: the employment based practicum and being familiar with social work practices.
least_helpful: i believe that every aspect of this work has been educational.
recommend_reason: casa for kids advocates for abused and neglected children in the foster care system. we work in a best practices model and exposed to a diverse population.
comments_supervisor: i was able to reach sally to discuss things and get feedback in between our weekly meetings if needed,

most_helpful: the experience with many different populations.
least_helpful: the logistics of the office were least helpful. we have to share offices due to lack of space and it can make scheduling difficult.
recommend_reason: it gave me wonderful experience and the work environment is really positive.
comments_supervisor: very knowledgeable and makes it easy to ask any questions.

most_helpful: the hands on learning experience, at times it was overwhelming but it helped me prepare for therapy.
recommend_reason: this environment was very helpful to grow and learn from other therapists as well as push myself out of my comfort zone.

most_helpful: the leadership and hands on experience.
recommend_reason: such a great experience.

most_helpful: the monthly staff meetings either in person or on zoom, the direct communication with the directors and other staff members through our app, and the training opportunities given.
least_helpful: the abrupt transition mid year of my afi.
recommend_reason: bgc was very accommodating to my work and school schedule.

most_helpful: the most helpful part of my practicum was learning from weekly meetings with my supervisor and learning about working within an integrated health care clinic.
least_helpful: i would say the least helpful or educational was the lack of clientele. during my time at the practicum there weren't as many patients as i would have liked to have seen individually for practice. during the beginner of the year there are less patients though which i understand.
recommend_reason: it is a great opportunity to learn how to work in an integrated health care clinic that serves migrant workers and the latinx population as well as rural populations.
comments_supervisor: melinda has a been a great supervisor who has plenty of experience as a clinical social worker and director of an organization in from previous jobs.

most_helpful: the most helpful was being able to do my practicum at my current place of employment and count hours that i was already working towards my practicum hours. since i am already in a counseling job, this made the content i was learning relevant to my actual day-to-day experience. it would have been a very different experience had i not been able to take what i was learning in classes and apply it to the work i am already doing.
least_helpful: i actually cannot think of a single unhelpful or educational aspect of my practicum. since i am already in my role as a college and career counselor and a member of the counseling team, i am surrounded by 3 high school counselors, 2 of whom have licensed to provide private counseling services and one social worker who was my afi. this made for a terrific experience for me and allowed me to get support as needed from a wide-range of sources.
recommend_reason: public schools are truly excellent sites for practicum work because they tend to have highly diverse students "clients" who bring their complex personal lives with them to school everyday. public schools allow for a unique experience that daily involves macro, mezzo and micro practice. they also function under the constraints of federal, state and local regulations which make navigating the systems not only complex because they require that those working within the systems whether they are in social work, counseling or teaching fields understand the implications of the regulations for their students as well as themselves. i have been able to work 1:1 with students and their families, in groups and approach complex problems with a systems level viewpoint. i have also been able to coordinate with outside agencies to help provide wrap-around services and support for my students and as a result, my community.
comments_supervisor: one of the issues of working in a public school is that there is no protected time, meaning that students always come first, especially students in crisis. this makes establishing protected windows for meeting incredibly challenging. however, this has not been an issue for me or my afi's over the last 2 years because we understand this and have had flexible approaches to connecting for supervision. sometimes this means meeting while we have door duty or as we walk through the halls. or, sometimes this means a debrief after a particularly challenging situation with a student or their family. i can imagine that it could be a frustrating experience for someone accustomed to keeping a rigid schedule to try and function and meet like this. this is not an afi problem in my case, it is a general time problem in schools that is compounded by union contracts etc, that make the prescribed hours of the day rigid while the proverbial wheels fly off the bus all day long making any kind of consistency difficult.
other_comments: as an existing employee, i feel i have been able to maximize my experience in a way that was truly valuable to me as well the school where i work. i realize many may not have this opportunity but for those that do have it as potential option, it would be fantastic if the um program made that clear from an admissions stand point. it was very difficult for me to ensure that an employment based practicum would be acceptable until after i was enrolled in the program. since employment based practicums are so valuable, making it more accessible from the beginning would likely encourage more people to engage in the practice. much of my support of existing employment practicums comes from the fact that i am already committed to the work i am doing and being able to take my practice to the next level because of the msw courses and practicum has allowed me to continually learning and adjusting my thinking through trail, error, reflection and support, which has only improved my professional practice and commitment.

most_helpful: the most helpful was my afi, jen certa.
least_helpful: my agency was unpredictable and constantly changing.
recommend_reason: i would recommend this if someone found the right afi, but the agency on a whole is driven by financials and not by quality of care.
comments_supervisor: jen certa is a phenomenal afi! she provided multidimensional learning opportunities for me which included research material, specific direction for challenges and barriers, macro level education information, and outstanding research based therapeutic and leadership skills.

most_helpful: the practicum allowed me the extra time to work with new employees on-boarding them into their work place. working with the new employees i was able to use new individual and group setting skills; motivational interviewing, assessment skills, and brainstorming with the new employee how to set goals for themselves. my observational skills i believe improved as well as my listening skills.
least_helpful: the least helpful, but much needed was the amount of time researching assessment tools, evaluation tools. the description was psychological safety in the work place and i needed to learn this type of human resources approach, discuss it with my afi, then plan how to implement objectives and tasks with offending the employee or allow the evaluation to lead to negativity.
recommend_reason: psychological safety in the workplace should be a regular practice for any business. to grow and to be successful required feedback from all employees. their voices will lend the administration and departmental leaders what types of improvements. this type of program is needed in all businesses, giving value and respect to your employee's voices.
comments_supervisor: hillary maxwell has such a strong diverse background and knowledge of psw. her therapeutic skills had me address some of my own issues allowing me to keep moving forward. she assigned tasks, such as researching evaluation tools that would help me in my practicum as well as benefit my workplace. she was always prepared for our weekly zoom meetings, using my learning agreement as her guide and keeping me on task.
other_comments: i truly appreciate my afi, hillary maxwell and my fl, kim garner. their knowledge, education, experiences, and their support allowed me to made a difference to a few new employees and i feel accomplished about that.

most_helpful: the supervising support and the training that it given to each student therapist.
least_helpful: the amount of support that was needed but it ended up being super helpful in my situation. it felt overwhelming some weeks going over 2 supervision days.
recommend_reason: provident is a crisis center that provides student with a diverse population to work with. you are able to create treatment plans, mental health assessments, receive real time feedback, and are able to give therapy under the supervision of a supervisor.

most_helpful: the trainings that they provide, either in person or online have been the most helpful to the educational process.

comments_supervisor: pam met with me once a week at a minimum, including both group supervision and individual supervision. additionally, she made herself available whenever possible to provide support for more immediate questions/concerns. she also fielded my personal concerns with specific patient interventions/business decisions and was transparent about management decision making.

most_helpful: i feel like the most helpful aspect of working in vpss was the ability to examine the systematic structure of the university system from a social work perspective. the individuals that i worked with did a wonderful job of explaining the system. they also invited me to participate in new program development within the university, which helped with my interdisciplinary skills.
least_helpful: there was not much room for interacting with individuals outside of the university system. i was able to meet those pieces of the learning agreement through a new program of the university. moving forward, however, i think it would be wise to set up specific goals for a new practicum student to meet those components of the learning agreement.
recommend_reason: awesome environment to learn about the structure of systems and understanding the amount of work that goes into how a university functions. i also learned a lot about interdisciplinary work. this placement provided me with a deeper understanding of the intricacies involved in systems work.

other_comments: this was a wonderful practicum year for me and i appreciate the people who supported me and worked with me to make this such a positive experience. i have detailed how these people and experiences are important to me above.

most_helpful: what was most helpful was woc's understanding of what um's msw program expected from a practicum. woc did a wonderful job of providing  multiple opportunities to meet the the 13 competencies in vastly creative ways. for example, my clinical year comprised of crisis counseling, pre-assessments to speculate the possibility sdmi diagnosis prior to having medicaid, interviewing potential clients at  msh and the state prison, as well as compiling and writing the rough drafts of continued stay reviews for clients in the adult group homes. there were also multiple opportunities of policy level experience involving several trips to the mt state capitol for advocacy work. winds of change was the perfect compliment to the msw program and has greatly aided in preparing me for micro, mezzo, and macro level social work that is in line with the 13 core competencies of social work.
least_helpful: woc is a work based practicum that pays you while still providing quality hours that fulfill the learning agreement requirements. however, for the last two months of my second semester, woc required that i worked 24 hours a week when i had previously only needed to work 20. that was incredibly stressful. however, with much advocation from management, the other practicum students and i were allowed to only work 15 quality practicum hours if we so chose. this is now the standard for incoming practicum students.
recommend_reason: winds of change is a great compliment to the msw program by aiding in the preparation of component micro, mezzo, and macro level social work. specifically, woc is very beneficial for anyone interested in getting their lcsw to work with people directly as well as cultivating a deep understanding of how to best serve people who struggle with mental health issues.
comments_supervisor: great having an afi who had gone through the um program and had a deep understanding of what would make the practicum meet the 13 competencies.
other_comments: very happy with my experience over all!

most_helpful: it wasn't very helpful but educationally i learned quickly the difference of having a philosophical difference with my practicum site and that no amount of experience will break through cultural unwillingness to change. social workers bear a pretty heavy responsibility to advocate inspite of the lack of support for the changes that they're advocating for.

other_comments: i strongly believe that if we want to be training professionals in this field and want them to behave as such they need to be in paid practicums where they're saddled with responsibility expectation and opportunity to expand their knowledge base but also practice what they're learning. no student should be working for free, we are already paying for school and it's a form of wage slavery to utilize students to fulfill the roles during internship and practicum while they're in school. furthermore the 20hr cap is unrealistic, it locks people into part time practicums longer, which affects their ability to take full time paid work. when most social workers work 40 + hours a week, and lcsws work between 40-50hrs a week its more realistic to expect students to have the opportunity to work 40hr a week jobs that cover their practicum requirements and able to actually use all 40hrs of their work for their practicum expectations. this would allow those that wish to finish their practicums sooner and get to work utilizing the experience from school sooner. students will get more and varied experience over the course of their professional career while in school. it will also serve to reduce the amount of debt that students incur while in school.

most_helpful: the residents that i worked with and the continued training opportunities provided.
least_helpful: the constant change of staff, my supervisor not having a degree in a social work adjacent field or super relevant job experience (often worked on the office side of things), and the lack of variety of trainings provided for staff.
recommend_reason: my supervisor and various coworkers consistently did not reflect the values being taught to me in my university courses nor did they seem to care about learning or developing these values. my supervisor seemed to be learning more from me and my education than i was from her, which felt a bit destabilizing.
comments_supervisor: my supervision frequently involved me answering questions my afi had around best practices, the values being taught to me in my courses, and the theories i was also learning about. while i was grateful she often engaged in my education it often came across as her being uninformed around various aspects of social work. along with this, she once spent an entire supervision asking me about my gender identity which not only caught me by surprise but also caused me to feel as though i couldn't say 'no' to engaging in this conversation. she essentially asked me to explain what 'non-binary' means, list the personal life experiences i have had with gender, and compared my experience to that of her gay son.
other_comments: i would not recommend that future students complete their practicum at the shirley miller attention home while margaret is still the program director there. while she seems to genuinely care about the clients her values seem to often be in contrast to that taught to us in our social work program. she seemed to be uninformed when it came to theories, best practices, and how to provide the best care to our residents while teaching her staff how to do so as well. staff frequently commented on the lack of support and/or education provided to staff and how out of their depth they often felt. the lack of cultural awareness, diversity, and general awareness of various issues impacting our residents' lives was jarring and at times caused me to question the safety and/or quality of life we provided.
